summary: "Allow non-space-administrators to create and share groups"
description: """In Confluence as a normal user I constantly have to remember lists of names for selectively sharing pages read/write permissions with groups of people, representing informal teams relating to the content and collaboration. I would like to be able to create a group, as I can in email contacts, that is just a shorthand for me to share with common informal groups, and add/remove members from that at any time. I do not want to have to create any administrator requests to be able to do this frequent operation.         In Jira I can create filters for private use and choose to share them with others. Likewise as well as being able to create private groupings of people for Confluence sharing and permissions, I would like to be able to share these with others. To avoid clutter they could be discoverable by others at one remove from the main/official space groups."""

summary: "Add customizable ""privacy"" and ""imprint"" Links in Footer"
description: """The European GDPR forces every platform provider of internet applications to provide proper privacy and imprint information individually on that platform.    We encountered that there is no easy way in Confluence to provide that Information.   As you as software provider need to keep us as customer your might assist us keep Confluence running by providing at least a simple ability to set custom """"privacy"""" and """"imprint"""" links on every Confluence site (I think in that footer where also """"about Confluence"""" and """"Report a Problem"""" and so on is linked is a good place). We could create websites to present this information.    A better approach would be to have the gdpr/privacy/imprint information to be customizable in Confluence admin interface and simply klickable. But afaik this might be a bigger change."""

summary: "Granular control over permissions Space admins can grant"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Space admins are giving other individual users admin privileges within spaces they control.    Permissions in this environment are managed using an automated tool (AGS) and it is not desired to grant permissions to individual users on the root of the space without approval or potentially at all.  h3. Suggested Solution    The desired outcome is to allow the Global admin to restrict a Space admin's ability to grant the """"admin"""" privilege of a space to individual users while still allowing the space admin to grant other permissions to individual users.    Stated differently, the desired ability is for the Global admin to remove the """"admin"""" privilege as an option for Space admin's to select. The Global admin would be the only one who can grant admin privilege at the space-level   h3. Why this is important    Global admins should have the ability to determine who can fill a space admin role, as individual space admins potentially give this right to users that should not have it.   The unauthorized admins then have rights not controlled via automation.  h3. Workaround    As a workaround, the Confluence administrator can hide the UI elements that are used to assign/remove the Space Admin permissions in the Space Tools page.   Note that this will not remove the functionality, but just hide the element so that users can't see these and, therefore, change its value.   The elements will be hidden from all users, also applying to Confluence administrators.   # Go to *Cog icon* > *General configuration* > *Stylesheet* to open the [_Global Stylesheet_|https://confluence.<USER>doc/styling-confluence-with-css-166528400.html] admin page.   # Click on the _Edit_ button, add the code below and then _Save_ it.   ** You may use any combination of the following options to hide one or multiple elements.      (!) If anyone needs to set the Space Admin permissions, then the <USER>must to remove this code from Confluence stylesheet."""
comments: "Hello Team,     On our Confluence environment, we are seeing several space admins giving individual permissions to other users as admins on the space.    We need a way on Confluence to block space admins to grant admins permissions to other users?    As we manage permissions using an automated tool we do not want to grant permissions to individual users on the root of the space.    We appreciate the effort here on this new feature. Thanks!" [SEP] "[~<USER> please see this possible workaround which may be of use to you:  h3. Workaround  As a workaround, the Confluence administrator can hide the UI elements that are used to assign/remove the Space Admin permissions in the Space Tools page.  Note that this will not remove the functionality, but just hide the element so that users can't see these and, therefore, change its value.  The elements will be hidden from all users, also applying to Confluence administrators.    # Go to *Cog icon* > *General configuration* > *Stylesheet* to open the [_Global Stylesheet_

summary: "Move Team Calendars expensive tasks to the Sandbox Framework"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Some Team Calendars operations are resource expensive and can interfere on other regular Confluence tasks.  This can lead to users experiencing overall slow downs on Confluence, which could be caused by punctual tasks.    h3. Suggested Solution  With the introduction of the [sandbox framework|https://community.<USER>t5/Confluence-discussions/Sandbox-Framework-for-Confluence-Server/td-p/1076306] we have now a way to move expensive processes to a separated JVM so that they don't interfere directly on Confluence performance.  For example, if a calendar export requires too much of the heap, it won't need to conflict with other regular Confluence operations for resources.    This suggestion is opened so that we can start to design Team Calendars on a way it can take advantage of the sandbox framework in any expensive task, making Confluence and TC more reliable and stable.     h3. Workaround   _None so far_"""

summary: "Drop matching licences policy asap, please!"
description: """Dear Atlassian,    I've been a fan of your tools for the last decade, and regard very positively the evolution in the products and growth of you offers.    However, the matching users licence policy is now completely outdate, and I strongly suggest you review it. Given the high number of plugins Confluence available, it happens quite often that we would like to start using a given plugin for on of our company's divisions only, limiting the licence (and price) of the plugin.    Due to your matching licences policy, this is not possible, which many times stops us from getting additional products complementing your tools.    IMHO, one of the main reasons for Atlassian's products success is the highly flexible and usable User Management approach, but oddly enough, the matching licences policy is a big obstacle to going deeper into the Atlassian ecosystem.    I therefore suggest you implement a more flexible licensing policy, where the system admin can 1) choose a sub-set of users from Confluence licence which have access to a given plugin, or 2) select all users if the licences match."""

summary: "Provide a feature that notifies users when a change in Confluence may affect the look of their content."
description: """Often times, various changes in Confluence in the form of HTML/CSS customizations, installation of third party plugins, may (unintentionally) produce unwanted changes in the way data/content is displayed in Confluence.     Often times, the end user does not know how/ or why the content that used to """"look"""" on way, suddenly look differently which causes frustrations, especially if the new look distorts the content significantly from how the author originally wants it to look like.     It would be great addition to Confluence if:  # Confluence would automatically detect that certain customisation changes _might_ affect the look and feel of a content and then notifies authors in this regards.   # This will help authors verify if there are any changes in the look of their content and as such help them either to edit the content to suite the new look or help them notify the admins of their respective Confluence instances that a recent customisation or plugin has caused their content to behave in a different way."""

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I would like to configure Space level redirect rules to forward users to a different instance"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When migrating Confluence Spaces from one instance to another, users may still access pages in the legacy instance without knowing the content was migrated and a more recent version is available.  Users may be directed to the legacy instance because of bookmarks or links available in external systems (not Confluence).  It would be interesting if we could adjust a rewrite rule directly in Confluence configuration to send users to the new instance when they are trying to read content from migrated Spaces.    h3. Suggested Solution  Provide the ability to configure directly in Confluence rewrite rules based on Spaces (Space key, for example) to send users to a new Confluence instance.     h3. Workaround   Review the options highlighted in [How to redirect Confluence users to a new URL|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-redirect-confluence-users-to-a-new-url-976758203.html]"""

summary: "Exceeding license limit should not render the server Read-Only for Data Center"
description: """h3. Problem Definition:    Data Center customers have an expectation of availability for their server instances. Often in these large data center environments, users are programmatically created or provisioned from directory systems. However, when the license count is exceeded, users can still continually be created. This is a problem, because once the license count is exceeded the server becomes Read-Only and is no longer available for any real use. This action negates the feature of high availability, since no work can be done on it.  h3. Suggested Solution:    No longer allow the creation of any new users that exceed the license limit, and keep the server from being made Read-Only.  h3. Why this is needed:    Keeping the license count from being exceeded in order to maintain high availability of the server for all existing users requires constant vigilance for some teams."""

description: """Most Cloud user lost the new editing experience after migrating the Cloud Site/Space to Server.   * [Confluence Editing Improvements|https://confluence.<USER>confcloud/confluence-editing-improvements-945107733.html]  While the content from Cloud is maintained and can be worked up on, the User experience is not good. Editing, especially of *Tables*, is cumbersome."""

The editor uses a different architecture and would be a significant change for our customers and our teams to roll out, implement and support.     If there are specific experiences/elements that you would like to see in Confluence Server and Data Center, please add this feedback against the closest suggestion for that component as there may be opportunities to do this as we work on features and consider alignments with the Cloud experience.    Thanks  Makisa | Senior Product Manager, Confluence Server and Data Center"

summary: "Slack workspace visibility should be restricted for space administrators in Confluence Server for Slack"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    With [Confluence Server for Slack (Official)|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1220186/confluence-server-for-slack-official?hosting=server&tab=overview], the list of all connected Slack workspace is visible in Space administration page. It should be restricted for space administrators.   !Screen Shot 2019-02-22 at 10.10.36.png|thumbnail!  h3. Suggested Solution    Add a permission configuration for workspace visibility.  h3. Why this is important    Workspace can be operated by different organization as well as Space in Confluence. Therefore Space administrator shouldn't be able to see what other workspaces exist."""

summary: "Confluence Supported Platforms states that AdoptOpenJDK is required"
description: """The Confluence Server [Supported Platforms|https://confluence.<USER>doc/supported-platforms-207488198.html] page states that Confluence can only be used with Oracle JRE/JDK or AdoptOpenJDK.  However, AdoptOpenJDK *is* OpenJDK...just a specific packaging of OpenJDK.    Requiring AdoptOpenJDK for the platform-native installers of Confluence Server might make sense from a packaging perspective.  However, for those of us using the """"TAR.GZ Archive"""" distribution and configuring our own installation, why is AdoptOpenJDK required?    Will Confluence Server actually fail with OpenJDK packaged in other ways, e.g. the Fedora distribution package?  If so, what are the impacts and when will they be resolved?  And if not, is this simply a documentation error?    Note that the Jira Server [Supported Platforms|https://confluence.<USER>adminjiraserver/supported-platforms-938846830.html] page states that OpenJDK is supported, and that testing has also occurred with AdoptOpenJDK.  Is this a more accurate phrasing of the Confluence Server requirement?"""

summary: "Official support for deploying Confluence Server on Kubernetes"
description: """In the interest of expanding the officially supported hosting options, it would be advantageous to have a tested and supported method/process for deploying Confluence on Kubernetes, as it's an option that's suited for hosting containerized applications and is therefore a good fit for running Confluence."""
comments: "Having got Confluence Data Center (cluster of 2 nodes) running on Docker Swarm, I thought it would be a good exercise to use Kubernetes as the management framework. I have tried running Confluence (Data Center - so I can run a cluster) on Kubernetes and the following is currently my blocker.    Whilst it is possible to mount a DFS share (!) into a container started by Kubernetes, for shared documents (using the fstab~cifs plugin), I am having great difficulty with providing persistent local storage to the containers.     Within my Docker installation, I had two mount points: local home directory and installation directory. I attempted to reproduce this approach for kubernetes, however it doesn't seem to work - and I think its due the order things get instantiated.    In lay-persons terms, Docker Swarm appears to do the following: create the container, mount volumes into the container, start the image - and so any external storage is visible when the image pull happens and the container then executes. Kuber

summary: "External Storage Integration"
description: """Confluence is widely used within our organisation and has become critical that we find a solution to attaching files in Confluence. We have limited our users to uploading X MB at a time however we would prefer that all attachments (including images embedded in pages) are saved automatically into our Document Management System. We have tested the SharePoint Connector for Confluence as advertised on the Atlassian marketplace however the functionality does not meet our requirements and it seems the vendor will not be releasing an update soon.         We understand the previous versions of Confluence catered for managing files using WebDav functionality and DB storage vs file system that we currently use. What is the roadmap for the use of external storage locations such as SharePoint?         We would really appreciate your assistance & guidance in determining how we can overcome this challenge in our organisation."""

summary: "Table editor collaboration request"
description: """Dear Confluence Cloud Team,    On behalf of the TechUp team I am reaching out to you asking for a collaboration request with your team around one of our Confluence apps Smart Tables currently on the Marketplace.  We believe that by building flawless and user-friendly experiences, we provide our customers the means to increase their teams' efficiency and engagement towards the Atlassian tools.    *Why Smart tables?*  Confluence users are eager to leverage native Confluence page editor's capabilities in order to perform their day to day tasks. Thus, building out tables in a few clicks by enriching the native confluence table using powerful operations and functions in the page editor experience is the main use case we addressed thanks to Smart tables.   As opposed to the other app's design and data scheme, we decided to lay the emphasis on a """"Confluence page editor"""" experience avoiding the use of macros, or importing an excel sheet in Confluence bringing therefore to the end-user the closest experience to the native confluence comfort level they are used to and more importantly an outstanding User experience by reducing the number of possible clicks to achieve their goal.   Smart tables has today more that 700 active instances on server in less of two years from the product launch and our customers shared the same feedbacks: """"ease of use"""" , """"user friendliness""""....    [check here|[https://marketplace-cdn.<USER>files/images/aaa9eafa-3d02-4b8d-af27-96c8c580664c.png]]         *New confluence editing experience:*  As a Marketplace vendor and fans of Atlassian toolset we totally support the company's growth shift to cloud-based services and products and are working towards this common objective.    The new editor of confluence pages, currently available in the Atlassian cloud relies on a React implementation of ProseMirror (the @atlaskit/editor). This new implementation does not allow us to interact with the editor instance in order for example to modify the content of cell when is formula is set.  That is a probably is a React / Component pattern which forces the state of the Editor component to be managed by the internal renderer. That is why, we would hope that you guys expose an Access point / bridge allowing extensions to be made on the new editor.      We are available to go through the technical details together maybe through a conference or any other format that is convenient for you.   We are looking forward to working together in order to support our customers with the new editor experience in the Atlassian cloud.    Cheers,"""

summary: "Use DNS for cluster peers - K8s support"
description: """Hi,    I am currently trying to run confluence data center nodes on Kubernetes.   Since the underlaying network layer does not support multicast, I am stuck with tcp_ip cluster.type.   As pods get assigned new IP-addresses on rebuild and I need to rebuild the pods in order to change the configuration, there seems to be a circular dependency I cannot get over.    As I see many people struggling with this exact part on K8s, I'd like to suggest the property of cluster.peers to be DNS names, a bit like CONFSERVER-53527, but without the synchrony-part.    The config-property would be extended like this:    The naive behavior-changes of the cluster-node discovery would consist of a simpe DNS-query while parsing the config, then recheck the DNS every time a node fails.    This change would directly enable Confluence to be run on Kubernetes.     """

summary: "Create bulk Jira issues from Confluence by specifying a common team field"
description: """This came in from a customer where we found a limitation of the Jira Issues macro.    The use case for """"appending to a specific team"""" - this is not only to relating this to a specific team but more a means to group all these together.    Right now we have a table in Confluence that we create Jira issues from (could be user requirements <USER> but there is no way to easily group these together as it may not need to be grouped by an epic but maybe a different items as these may only be part of the stories in an epic but want to still group these together.    In our example there is are numerous steps to rollout a process across a team so this is a template of work in Confluence which we then create per team. When creating these story's in Jira there is no way to note which team this is. So would be great if we could reference a field of information like Team name that can the show up on the created Jira issue, as opposed to a column on a table that needs to be populated for every row.    Instead of specifying an Epic to associate these tickets with or using a column, I'd like to be able to either specify a bulk item arbitrarily or have some more options when creating bulk issues."""
comments: "This would be great it it grouped by a common avenue - sometimes a team, sometimes a different attribute.  This would be a great feature if you having living requirements in Confluence that you want to create Jira Issues for <USER> [SEP] "This would be a big help, a case in point is when I start an issue in Confluence I very limited in the amount of fields I can populate." [SEP] "Currently I can only populate two or three OOTB fields using confluence table to create Jira issues, it is limitation to build a good story using both Jira and Confluence.    Vote for this enhancement to support customized Jira fields."

summary: "Ability to disable the share page with groups feature"
description: """Currently, the [Page Sharing|https://confluence.<USER>doc/share-a-page-or-blog-post-16482511.html] option in Confluence is controlled by the Share Page add-on {{Manage add-ons > System > Share Page}}, however, it doesn't seem to be possible to disable the sharing with users, groups or mail.     It would be great to split those sharing options, so if needed, we can disabled them individually. """
comments: "For us, the issue is allowing page sharing with groups.  We need the ability to disable that functionality based on the following scenario:    If Confluence is sync'd with an LDAP server such as Active Directory, mailing lists are pulled into Confluence as groups, with the members of the group being the emails that are part of the mailing list.  AND, if a user shares a page (possibly unintentionally) with a group that represents a LARGE mailing list, they could end up spamming thousands of users.  Many of those users may not even use Confluence, and thus have no idea of the context, and that could result in a significant number reporting the email as spam and even a possible phishing scam.    Email servers can have restrictions in place to stop this type of wide-spread email, but this scenario in Confluence would bypass those restrictions because it sends thousands of emails to individual users, instead of one email to the mailing list that represents the group the page is being shared with.    So, at the

summary: "Update the REST API to allow for more endpoints to match previous offerings."
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Since Confluence 5.5, previous Remote APIs, such as JSON-RPC and XML-RPC, have been deprecated and are no longer supported. However, many of the methods available through these API endpoints have not been ported over to the REST API, which is now being utilized.    This can cause issues for any users that were utilizing these older endpoints and are unable to make the switch to the REST API.    While it's technically possible for users to still use the older API endpoints,  it's not specifically stated if these endpoints will be made available in the REST API or if at all, and is also another concern.    h3. Suggested Solution    Create equivalent endpoints in the REST API to match what is available through the older Remote APIs.    h3. Workaround    Depending on the specific functions needed, the Confluence CLI can provide many of the same functionality, but it is a paid third-party add-on."""

summary: "Article meta information - Friendly name and hidden tags"
description: """It would be useful to be able to store a configurable friendly name and a list of friendly tags for each article.    This information should only be accessible and configurable by space admins and above.    The goal is to be able to use this 'meta' information about the articles in the design and structure of information across our wikis.    The friendly name and friendly tags would be added to an article's Friendly Details screen, accessed through the standard page menu, and only available to those with space admin privileges or higher.    *Friendly name*    In particularly large spaces, there is often the requirement to name article with over-long names to in order to ensure uniqueness across the space and also to heavily contextualise the info, such as a single space containing the details for multiple tools used on a single project. Each article pertaining to a single tool requires the tool's name prepended to ensure uniqueness. However, in the body of the relevant tool's articles, this extra naming is actual inconvenient, unnecessary, and occassionally intrusive.    A 'friendly name' would allow information designers to dispense with the extra naming baggage, and create articles that read better.    I'm aware of the ability to set custom text while including links in articles, but that text once changed breaks the logical link with the article and can forever be missed when updates are made to the article directly.    The friendly name allows for a single point of update, while options to use the article's title or the article's friendly name in links, children displays, content by label, and other macros give us many more options.    *Friendly tags*    Labels and tags are a great way to categorise and gather articles together, not least of all with the plethora of macros available but also in searches.    However, labels as currently implemented have two drawbacks:   # They are permanently visible on the page, which may not be appropriate for certain categories (secret projects, keywords, <USER>).   # There is no specific permission to gate access to labels. Access to the page is all that is needed to add labels or even remove labels, some of which are invaluable to the organisation of information.    Friendly tags, added and maintained in the same screen as the friendly name, would be hidden to all other than space admins and above. They would remain unmolested from accidental deletion or unintentional hijacking by others who innocently reuse a 'restricted' or administrative label on their own content.         Both these friendly features give content managers the confidence to arrange their content free from fear of their efforts being interfered with, however innocently, by other wiki users."""

summary: "Awareness of restricted content"
description: """Restriction feature in Confluence could be enhanced with ability to let users actually see restricted content exist and therefore request access if it seems relevant for them.    Today, restricted content is not visible at all for all users which are not authorized and they are only aware of its existence when they have page URL and they can't open the content. But, in some cases, it will be helpful to directly present links to such content in page tree (ideally with a pad lock in front of Title to directly inform content is indeed restricted for given user), so user can easily request access to content owners.    *Reasons :*   * Broader awareness of restricted content (Spaces or sections are not """"empty"""" or """"missing info"""", users simply don't have access to content due to restrictions)   * Avoidance of restricted content duplicates (Users are recreating similar content thinking it doesn't exist)   * Ease access request as users can directly see content which is indeed restricted for them.   * Ability to distinguish restricted content for limited users (not visible at all) and restricted content for larger audience (links in page tree).    *Limits:*   * It should remain possible to not show restricted content titles in Page tree.     *Look'n feel*    An extra option in current restriction drop down menu.    """"Limited viewing and editing restricted - Everyone can view Titles in Page tree, only some can fully view and edit"""" to be placed 3rd position between """"Editing restricted"""" and """"Viewing and editing restricted"""".     """

summary: "Provide REST API methods to manage users and groups"
description: """h3. Summary    As a Confluence <USER>I would like to be able to manage users and groups directly from REST API endpoints. Automation and scripting of user management can be made easier with REST API.  h3. Problem statement    User and groups management is currently not possible via REST API only. Bulk operations, automation or scripting of management actions is not currently possible when using REST API. User management was previously possible with SOAP, which has been deprecated.  h3. Suggested Resolution    Provide methods akin to what as present with SOAP that would allow Admins to manage users and groups via REST API.                   _Previous Ticket Description_   As Confluence is popular tool used by many users, it should provide the API to add users to groups, similar to what Jira Cloud has."""

summary: "Allow Synchrony to be hosted externally in Confluence Server"
description: """h3. Problem Statement    Currently, only Confluence Data Center has the option of either hosting Synchrony externally (standalone) or using the internal Synchrony proxy (managed by Confluence).   * [Possible Confluence and Synchrony Configurations for Confluence Data Center|https://confluence.<USER>doc/possible-confluence-and-synchrony-configurations-958779064.html#PossibleConfluenceandSynchronyConfigurations-PossibleconfigurationsforConfluenceDataCenter]    h3. Suggestion    It would be great if Confluence Server could also have the same option so administrators can setup a separate server and have its own resources strictly for Synchrony."""

summary: "Limit the number of document conversion threads by default"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Confluence uses document conversion service to convert Office file and PDF files for preview and thumbnail generation. Currently, this service has a queue of tasks to run through and the file conversion service spins up background threads to convert those documents. Unfortunately, if one document is taking a bit more time to convert, Confluence might spin too many concurrent threads which might add too much pressure on the CPU and memory.    h3. Suggested Solution  The suggested solution is to limit the number of concurrent conversion threads by default.      h3. Workaround  The current workaround is to add the following JVM argument to limit the number of conversion threads.  """

description: """h3. Issue Summary    New templates are featured in the Atlassian product post for Cloud.   *  [https://www.<USER>blog/confluence/new-templates-for-confluence]  *  [https://www.<USER>software/confluence/templates]    It came to our server customer's attention, and they're looking for these templates. A workaround will be added here when available."""

We spend a great deal on the product, and were surprised to find something like templates is limited only to the Cloud subscription users. summary: "Improve 'Restrict a Calendar' documentation to explain that calendar owners cannot be restricted"
description: """h3. Issue Summary    Team Calendars' '[Restrict a Calendar|https://confluence.<USER>teamcal/restrict-a-calendar-660737259.html]' documentation explains how to restrict viewing and/or editing.    However, the documentation does not make clear that the Restrictions were designed in a way that the calendar creator has the privilege to keep control over the calendar even if the restrictions are modified, which can cause confusion for the customers ([CSP-261644|https://getsupport.<USER>browse/CSP-261644]).    This mean they can always edit/view a calendar they created, change its name, change the related space, <USER>  h3. How to Improve    Although this is a design choice to avoid other users taking full control of calendars they have not created, the inclusion of a paragraph explaining that calendar creators cannot be restricted helps to clarify this."""

summary: "Inline Code"
description: """The fact that this isn't supported out of the box is crazy to me.  There *really* needs to be a way to support inline code.           I know that there are alternatives out there.  Tons of people are out there suggesting creating custom CSS blocks and all sort of other things like simply using monospaced text.         Let me be clear, as a developer who's company is forcing me to use Confluence for documentation, this one thing makes me completely dislike the product.  I want to type some stuff between two back-ticks and have it let the reader know that this is code/command.      Anything less than this for a technical documentation tool is unacceptable."""
comments: "This feature needs to be implemented ASAP.  I am working on implementing Confluence in our organization and I can already see the lack of an inline code feature being a huge problem. "

summary: "remove required label from page property report macro"
description: """Since cql was introduced, a lot of macros rely on cql-conditions. The page property report macro does as well. A powerful tool was introduced with cql to express conditions. Though I can use a lot of diverse data to describe filtering I am still forced to use a label - even if I have other structural data like metadata by Communardo for example.  It would be sufficient just to rely on metadatasets, but I am forced to make sure that metadatasets and labels are adressing the same pages. I have two structural criterias describing the same content.    If the label wouldn't be necessary to make page property report output a list then it would decrease a lot of effort to synchronize and double our information architecture.    Please make parameter label not required or better allow free use of cql to to describe content whose page properties have to be shown."""

summary: "Separate concepts of Creator and Author (and owner)"
description: """If I look at a Confluence article, it says 'Created by So-and-so at whatever-time.' If I look at the article's Page Description, it lists 'So-and-so' as the author of the page.    One use case where this is problematic is migrating data from other systems. If Mr. So-and-so is tasked with getting pages from the old system in to Confluence, *they do not become the author of the work they are reproducing.* As such, they require the ability to assign authorship. They can remain the page's creator because they created the page, but they did not author the work in that page.   # The creator of a migrated page is not necessarily the author (you do not become the author of something when you reproduce it -- ask a copyright lawyer)   # The owner of a page is not necessarily the author (the author could've left the company, but they're still the author)    So all of these concepts need to be clearly separated. Making creator/author the same by default will maintain the current behaviour. Adding the ability to assign an author will cover use cases where someone is tasked with migrating existing data into Confluence. Such a function should have associated permissions controls so that it is available to trusted members of a team.    There are various workarounds such as directly changing the database or using a macro to contradict what Confluence is telling you, but Confluence's job is document management. Having to do unsupported things to the db or having to jury rig a solution to track the most basic meta-data associated with a document makes me want to say words that would be counter-productive. So I'll leave it there.    Thank you for your time and consideration,"""
comments: "No-brainer.    In the Cloud version hacking in the database is no option.    I do not want to be an owner for life, just because I imported the pages, nor for the fact that I created pages for others to assist and continue.     To be notified of changes of 'your' page is annoying.    And if pages are imported form somewhere else (like after a company splitting up) you don't want to remain with pages owned by an 'alien'.     " [SEP] "{quote}I do not want to be an owner for life, just because I imported the pages, nor for the fact that I created pages for others to assist and continue.   {quote}  Amen. As an admin who creates tons of pages for others to help get them started, I can attest it is super annoying. We have people who end up copying pages, breaking links, <USER> because they want to be the creator. It's a mess. " [SEP] "As an admin I also have the problem where I create a space of just a page for someone who then takes it over, restricts it and yet I continue to get requests and queries relating

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I would like to configure a timeout specific to long running user macros"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  An [User Macro|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/writing-user-macros-964961202.html] is a great way to extend Confluence functionalities, since the administrator can code virtually anything.    Depending on what the macro is coded for, its operation may take a long time to complete and, while it is running, it is prone to impact on Confluence performance.  Currently, there's no mechanism to kill the inner operation performed by the user macro if it is running for longer than a threshold.    h3. Suggested Solution  Introduce a configurable timeout to user macros and an option to asynchronously run them.  If a macro is taking a long time to complete, kill the macro rendering thread after the specified threshold.    Although the macro should be extensively tested for performance and security reasons, it would be great if Confluence could safeguard itself from expensive operations.     h3. Workaround   _No workaround so far."""

Currently, there is only a private user-specific API to query the task of the current logged in user.    /rest/mywork/1/task         I would like to have such a list for all users, or for selected users or groups.    I would like to close, update specific tasks with the API."""

summary: "As an administrator I would like to configure HTTP2 support in Confluence's Tomcat level"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  [HTTP/2|https://developers.google.com/web/fundamentals/performance/http2/] is designed to address several limitations found in HTTP/1.1.  Although Tomcat 9.X (and 8.5.X) supports HTTP/2, it can only be enabled with [Apache Portable Runtime (APR)|http://tomcat.apache.org/native-doc/], which is not natively compiled with bundled Tomcat in Confluence.    h3. Suggested Solution  Bundle Confluence with the necessary artifacts to enable HTTP/2 out-of-the-box and provide the necessary documentation to configure this.  Also, Confluence should be fully tested to work seamlessly with HTTP/2, as well as make use of some functionalities such as _push promise_ to improve application's performance.    h3. Why this is important  Full benefits of HTTP/2 can only be achieved if it is enabled all the way through the network, although you can get most of it by terminating HTTP/2 on a web server.  Also, it would be important to have HTTP/2 working when there's no web server in front of Confluence.    h3. Workaround   The supported workaround is to terminate SSL in a web server, such as NGINX and Apache, and configure it to accept HTTP2 connections."""

summary: "Epics - Display percentage complete to easily assess progress"
description: """Jira's epic tab has a similar graph, but we want to see a percentage complete in Confluence for epics. (i.e. Profile Page - 56% complete)    The best I can do currently is to create a JQL filter based on the number of issues not resolved in an epic vs total issues in an epic, and then manually do the math.     This is simple arithmetic, & I wish there was a way to show percentage complete for epics. We have our roadmaps and other filters in Confluence, & this functionality would make them stronger reporting tools for stakeholders.         There are two places 3 ways I could imagine this being implemented in Confluence working :  # In the Jira Issue/Filter macro under display settings   # As its own macro    I've also added a suggestion as Show to where it could live in Jira: https://jira.<USER>browse/JRASERVER-67438  # Next to the 'Issues in Epic' label in Jira side of the  """

summary: "Improve docker container behavior when moving/copying a Confluence instance"
description: """Currently, when installing Confluence using Docker, it will start a new installation from scratch, which is not desirable in cases on which the end goal is to move an existing installation (or clone a Confluence instance, to create a test instance).    It would be useful to provide an option to start the Docker container with an already finished installation (with all of the settings already present on the existing {{confluence.cfg.xml}} file), as it would simplify this process."""
comments: "I'd like to restore a backup of production Confluence to a staging environment, restart the staging docker container, and have it sort out correctly updating/replacing confluence.cfg.xml itself based on env variables. Rather than having to manually edit the file.         The documentation specifies a small set of clearly defined changes to make - it'd be ideal if this was automated and baked into the image."

summary: "Provide an ability to upgrade third party libraries like Jquery seperately from Confluence "
description: """*Scenario*   There are many third-party libraries used in Confluence which might be affected by some security vulnerabilities. In many of these cases, they are resolved in the later version of these libraries.   Generally, we would have to wait for the next Confluence upgrade that will contain these updated libraries to mitigate these security risks.    *Suggestion*   Confluence should provide the ability to upgrade third party libraries like Jquery separately i.e without the need to upgrade the Confluence itself. This would help a lot in mitigating security risks and also prevent delays in waiting for the next Confluence version containing the updated libraries."""

summary: "Native Linux Companion App"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Since Confluence 6.11 the [Edit Files|https://confluence.<USER>conf613/edit-files-964960723.html] feature works with the [Atlassian Companion app|https://confluence.<USER>doc/atlassian-companion-app-release-notes-958455712.html].    The Companion App is available for Mac and Windows, but we don't have a release for Linux distributions.    h3. Suggested Solution  Provide a release of the Companion App to Linux distributions.    h3. Why this is important  Confluence users may choose to run their personal computers on Linux. This may be true for many developers, which might be the heavy users of Atlassian tools and Linux distributions.  The [Edit in Office|https://confluence.<USER>conf65/office-connector-prerequisites-939701141.html] functionality used to support Linux with Open Office.    h3. Workaround     As a workaround for now, while this is not a native solution, Confluence administrators may want to check the [GoEdit|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1211160/goedit-simply-edit-attachments?hosting=server&tab=overview] App by <USER>    *Not tested*:  Linux users might want to try installing the Companion App using [WINE|https://www.winehq.org/]."""

summary: "Prevent ""Watch a few spaces"" dialogue from suggesting personal spaces"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  The """"watch a few spaces"""" dialogue is triggered when a user logs into Confluence for the first time.    This is based on popular spaces, and can sometimes contain personal spaces.    As a result, users are watching the suggested personal space, furthering its popularity, which is undesirable in many cases.    h3. Suggested Solution  Allow users to prevent personal spaces from being listed here, or block them altogether.    h3. Why this is important  Many customers don't need their users watching personal spaces, but the more relevant spaces."""

summary: "Make AWS Clustering IAM role and access key optional"
description: """In confluence.cfg.xml it is required to have either confluence.cluster.aws.iam.role or confluence.cluster.aws.iam.key specified.    If neither one is specified, then catalina.out will log:  {quote}com.atlassian.config.bootstrap.BootstrapException: Unable to bootstrap application: Exception bootstrapping cluster:Error bootstrapping cluster (error.cluster.aws.iam.role.and.access.key.not.defined)  {quote}  I believe Hazelcast can default to using the IAM Role assigned to the EC2 Instance, but the ConfluenceConfigurationListener component is not allowing for this functionality to occur.    This suggestion would just help administrators simplifying configurations and allow for more flexibility in their configuration options.    Note: I am not and cannot use the cloudformation provided by Atlassian for deploying Conflunce Data center in AWS."""

summary: "Confluence Redundancy"
description: """As confluence becomes critical to organization, its important to have this available all the time. However, for any reason there might be a unplanned downtime and business gets affected. Natively, Confluence does not have mirroring concept. The request is to build a native capability in Confluence to mirror entire site or spaces in read-only format (ideally data should be available to the mirror site in real time). This is not a DR site but a redundant site always available to users irrespective of main confluence availability.     """

summary: "Confluence 6.13 and 6.14 EAP - New Search in Theme apps"
description: """Hi Atlassian     We are testing the new Search Experience in 6.13 and 6.14 and noted it is not compatible with Enterprise Theme and other themes available in the Atlassian Marketplace. The new search bar simply does not appear when clicking into the search bar or entering something.    We would like to make our Enterprise Theme compatible and have the new search experience as seamlessly as possible integrated into our Enterprise Theme to give our Confluence users the best experience. Other vendors supplying Themes probably face the same need.     Please supply resources containing informations for app developers how to integrate the new search in theme apps.    Are there for example Java Script Events needed to call the search?    We are without any documentation at the moment and are unable to integrate our theme with the new search. As Confluence 6.14 is on the horizon, we want to provide full compatibility as soon as possible.           Please provide documentation for app developers. Your help is appreciated.    Niklas  bitvoodoo     """

summary: "Additional License Tiers"
description: """We are in aged care in Australia with 17 facilities.  Most of our staff are not full time. They work shifts.  In fact it takes around 3 staff to equal one full time equivalent.    We are using confluence and are planning it to be our corporate platform but we cannot justify the cost of moving from 2000 user tier to 10000 user tier knowing that the named user base may only be around 3000 which roughly equals around 1200 full time staff.    Can you please set up user tiers between the 2000 and 10000 that would enable businesses such as ours to enable confluence for all our staff."""

summary: "Create checkboxes / @mentions without creating tasks"
description: """Some of our teams use checkboxes on Confluence pages along with @mentions to determine whether or not each individual is present at the meeting. In this case, it doesn't make a whole lot of sense to have these lines show up under the """"Tasks"""" page for each user - it really just clutters up the tasks, making them more difficult to efficiently use.    It would be nice to be able to have an @mention and a checkbox in the same line of text on a Confluence page without it creating a task. Perhaps a standard """"checkbox"""" component rather than just a task component. Alternatively, if there was a way to select a task declaration, then click an onHover link (or something similar) to remove it from the task list, that would work.    Thanks!"""

summary: "Confluence to Confluence integration (different instances)"
description: """h1. Use case    As I user working in a company with multiple distinct confluence instances, I would like to do perform:   * Cross instance linking   * Cross instance attachment use (i.e. for images)   * Cross instance macro usage   ** Task reporting (_Task report_ macro)   ** Page properties reporting (_Page properties report_ macro)   ** _Page tree_ macro   ** Cross instance page excerpts (_Excerpt Include_ macro)   ** Basically all macros that do reporting    h1. Precedence    This use case for Jira to Jira, Confluence to Jira and Jira to Confluence is already supported as all this cases support multiple instances with regards to integration  h1. User interest    I have collected already existing questions for this requirement on atlassian forum under the label [confluence-to-confluence-integration|https://community.<USER>t5/tag/confluence-to-confluence-integration/tg-p]    Also, CONFSERVER-24425 relates to this very same use case, but it was closed as answered, and the linked issue that is a follow up is a broken link"""
comments: "The feature would be extremely helpful for our organization. We have several server based Confluence instances and need a possibility to embed content from one instance to another one. Since there is no possibility to use iframes for this without security implications described in [https://confluence.<USER>confkb/confluence-page-does-not-display-in-an-iframe-827335781.html?_ga=2.50003430.1537957449.1588688839-2131708736.1551689076] , we are currently getting by by using simple web links to refer to the required content, which is not an optimal solution.    What we need is something similar to the Jira macro in Confluence, which can embed issues from an integrated Jira. Something like an include page macro from another Confluence, where an application link has been established.    We a thrilled to find out if we could use this feature in future!" [SEP] "I would add the following to the above list:    Allow excerps to be replicated or used between instances    Allow page inclusions as well."

summary: "Confluence Server Mobile App Read-Only Mode"
description: """h3. Problem    We would like to restrict what users can do in the Confluence Server mobile app. As it stands, there is no way to change what users can and cannot do within the mobile app. In particular, we want to restrict:   * The option to download attachments   * The option to copy and paste contents    h3. Suggested Solution    _Add a read-only mode to the Confluence Server mobile app._   h3. Why This Is Important    _This is important to our users with security concerns, or who wish to restrict what can be done while using the Mobile App. The users would like to allow the use of the app, but to restrict certain features so they are only available within Confluence._      """

summary: "Categorizing the Spaces in confluence"
description: """Hi,    Just like in Jira, we have a feature of adding the project into categories, We should have the options for categories in Confluence itself.     Currently, we have the option of labels in Confluence to categorize the projects, but (depending on the requirement- can be considered as drawback or advantage) we can add multiple labels on single space. Rather we can have the feature of adding category to a space and there cannot more than one category for single space.          Thanks    <USER>""

summary: "Add named user roles to space and page permissions"
description: """Jira supports named user roles for projects, which helps simplify the process of assigning permissions to different users. With so many different permissions on Confluence spaces, it gets annoying having to copy the relevant permissions to each user or group based on how that user should use the space. Then, when the names are all sorted alphabetically, it's hard to tell who plays what role within the space: who are the """"admins"""", who are the """"editors"""", who are the """"viewers"""", <USER>    I would like to ask that a new named role feature be added to Confluence so that I can manually create roles for a given space, such as """"Viewer"""" or """"Editor"""", assign permissions to that role, then assign roles to users. It would also follow that the same roles for a space be available for restricted pages withing the space, effectively acting like a custom group available only within that space. This feature could exist alongside the existing permissions structure, in case people prefer the current approach.    A workaround is to create a global group within Confluence (or the upstream directory server(s)), e.g. """"Custom Space XYZ Editors"""" and assign permissions to that group, but that requires admin input and pollutes the global group namespace. Instead, named roles within a space's permissions would not have either of these problems."""

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I would like to know if any App would be affected when disabling another App or a module of another App"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When disabling a Confluence App (aka plugin or add-on), Confluence will automatically disable any other App that has a strong dependency on the former (or a module of it) with no warning messag in the UI.  For example, when disabling _Confluence Jira Plugin_, _confluence-software-blueprints_ will be disabled because the latter has a strong dependency on the {{jqlHelper}} module of the former.    h3. Suggested Solution  Before disabling an App, Confluence should check if any other has a dependency on it.  If there's any, then Confluence should advise that disabling _App A_ will cause _App B_ to be disabled.    h3. Why this is important  There's no clear definition on Apps' dependency on the Confluence UI when administering Apps.  That means Confluence admin might take an action without knowing the full consequences of it.     h3. Workaround   _no workaround so far_"""

You cannot use a developer license on an H2 database, only a trial license per the [documentation|https://confluence.<USER>doc/embedded-h2-database-145098285.html].    Administrators would like to test non-DC functionality using the H2 database on a local machine, similar to how the trial licenses can be used with an H2 database.    As a workaround you can use a trial license from https://my.<USER>"""

summary: "Edit Page Restrictions through the API"

This is possible in Confluence Cloud, per the [Confluence Cloud API documentation|https://developer.<USER>cloud/confluence/rest/?_ga=2.242477058.1405191471.1549894744-1705418969.1518219027#api-content-id-property-key-delete].     h3. Suggested Solution  Implement a similar method/endpoint for the Confluence Server API.    h3. Why this is important  If administrators need to update a large number of page restrictions, the API would be helpful for automating some of this work.     h3. Workaround   The [<USER>Confluence CLI|https://bobswift.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CSOAP/overview] can achieve this objective.     """

summary: "Confluence support for Zing OpenJDK"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently AdoptOpenJDK is supported as an Oracle JDK alternative ([Is OpenJDK Supported by Confluence?)|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/is-openjdk-supported-by-confluence-297667642.html]   Customers potentially will want to use other OpenJDK solutions moving forward.     h3. Suggested Solution  Please consider support for other OpenJDKs, specifically:   Zing OpenJDK [https://www.azul.com/products/zing]    h3. Why this is important  Other OpenJDK solutions will be used by customers    h3. Workaround  Only supported option at this time is AdoptOpenJDK"""

summary: "Ability to disable automatically inherited Page Restrictions"
description: """h4. Problem Definition  Currently, there is no way to disable the automatic permissions that are inherited on child pages, from the configured page restrictions on parent pages. This is a limitation in select cases where a user might not want those permissions to be automatically applied in the first place.    h4. Suggested Solution  The ability to simply select whether restrictions from a parent page should be applied to its child pages would already solve this issue, and provide greater freedom to Confluence admins."""
comments: "Once a space has already become large and relatively complicated, moving some pages to a separate space to accommodate separate permissions is not efficient.      Ideally, I would want the ability to _DENY_ access to a group or individual, so I could override the inherited permissions _just for that group/individual and just for that page._    Being able to disinherit parent permissions for that page (would this affect the sub-pages?) at least gives us the ability to virtually ""deny"" by only adding groups/individuals we want to ALLOW.  However, that is also not efficient if you have an environment that has dozens of groups and you only want to exclude _one_ of them on that page. :-/     "

summary: "Enable multiple Confluence instances to connect via Slack Server plugin"
description: """As an administrator with multiple Confluence instances, I would like to be able to connect multiple Confluence instances to a single Slack Workspace.   [*Confluence Server for Slack (Official)*|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1220186/confluence-server-for-slack-official?hosting=server&tab=overview]  h3. Why is it important  Environments may rely on multiple instances for day to day activities and notifications from all instances are required.    h3. Workaround  None"""

summary: "Extension point to filter information about created pages on Confluence dashboard"
description: """Hi Team!    My name's <USER>and I'm a product owner of [Smart Attachments for Confluence|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1219123/smart-attachments-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview] app.    It will be nice to have an extension point to filter appearance of pages on the Confluence dashboard. So that we will have an option to block appearance of newly created pages (created by some third-party app) on the dashboard.    At the moment we are setting the old dates to pages, so they do not appear. But this is not convenient and is not good in general from the architecture perspective.    *Use Case*    We are using your native Confluence pages to render our own entities (folders) in the space storage for keeping documents and files. The problem is that a newly created page is displayed on Confluence dashboard. We would greatly appreciate if you could provide/implement some extension point to filter information about created pages on Confluence dashboard. So that we can use the native functionality instead of tricking Confluence with dates."""

summary: "Separate Plugin Administration permission from System Administrator permissions"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently, in order to administer Confluence plugins, users need to have <USER>Permissions.    If a Confluence administrator needs to allow some users to administer only the Confluence plugins, giving those users <USER>permissions will give them total control over the Confluence instance, which is inconvenient.  h3. Suggested Solution    Separate the permission to administer plugins from <USER>permissions.    This way, users can be authorized to administer plugins without having permission to the remaining General Configurations (*Configuration*, *Users & Security*, *Look and Feel* and *Administration*).  h3. Workaround    Currently there is none."""

summary: "Confluence Excerpt That Populates Jira Description"
description: """We generally have a process of creating Epics in Jira and linking them to a Confluence page that contains the detailed requirements (i.e. prior to create the Jira tickets for the work). It would be nice to have the ability to have an Excerpt type macro in confluence where you can link the excerpt to a Jira ticket and have that information displayed in the ticket. This would prevent the typical cut-and-paste problems and keeping the data in-sync. While, in principle, I think of this as being linked into the Jira Description field, it may make sense to have it as a separate field. I do realize this poses a problem, because if you have more than one Excerpt linked to a single Jira ticket, what would be the best way to display that information. I would envision it as a page tool, where the most recently updated excerpt is show on the ticket with navigation buttons to swipe through the excerpted segments. Overall, the idea is really to have a Jira ticket that has tight integration to a single Confluence page.  """

summary: "Allow restriction of user results from showing up in quick search and rest API searches for certain users or groups"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Even after [restricting a user's ability to use the people directory or perform a quick search|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-restrict-users-from-accessing-people-directory-or-search-users-from-quick-search-688883545.html], a user can still use the REST API to locate another user's information:      h3. Suggested Solution  We cannot restrict the API entirely as Confluence uses the same REST API for it's processes as noted in [CONFSERVER\-7913: Need ability to limit use of remote API to certain users, or a certain group|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-7913]. However, it would be useful to be able to restrict certain users or groups from receiving user results.    We have the [CONFSERVER\-7837: People Directory and """"controlled"""" privacy / configure access to People Directory depending on Group membership|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-7837] feature request, however, this feature doesn't include results that can be pulled from the REST API."""

summary: "Option to disable Launch On Startup during installation of Atlassian Companion app"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    During installation, there is no option to disable Launch on Startup for Atlassian Companion App. for both .exe and .msi packages. Only once it is launched, we can disable the option.    h3. Suggested Solution  * Add an option during installation, where this can be disabled.  * For MSI packages, we can implement something similar to Roaming profiles that can be configured deploying through MSI package, we can try to add an option for this configuration as well.    h3. Why this is important  The customer has complained that they don't want the Companion app to automatically launch for all users. As many users don't need or use this app, it creates unnecessary confusion for the users and also cause drain on resources.    h3. Workaround   (Optional)  After first launch of the app, the user can disable the Automatic Launch on Startup.  """
comments: "We have reviewed this suggestion and decided that we will not implement it. "

summary: "A new approach for resource-intensive tasks in Standalone Server"
description: """On Confluence 6.10, we made awesome improvements, one of the major improvements regarding performance, was made uniquely for Data Center version of Confluence, [a sandbox|https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-6-10-release-notes-952058221.html#Confluence6.10ReleaseNotes-Anewapproachforresource-intensivetasksinDataCenter] to handle such processes. It's known that aspose and file previews can cause severe performance issues.     This suggestion is to add the same feature for Confluence Server"""

summary: "Limit which groups users can share content with"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    AD groups inherited through a user directory connection such as the """"Everyone"""" group will have all users as members. It is desired to block a group such as this from being used to share content with, i.e. the group does not show up as a sharing recipient and therefore cannot be used to spam all users.   h3. Suggested Solution    Create the ability to blacklist groups which will suppress their ability to be used for sharing purposes, but will leave them available for user security/permissions purposes. The could also be done via group attribute that is either """"can be used in sharing"""" = on or off  h3. Workaround    An LDAP filter can be put in place to prevent the Everyone group from being inherent through the user directory connection, but this takes away the groups usefulness as a security/permissions group in Confluence."""

summary: "Ability to track the user login attempt"
description: """As a Confluence administrator, we would like to have a way to track the user login attempt for security reasons. It will be great to have the similar as in JIRA:       *Workaround solution:*  You can still enable the debug logging for the Default login manager package by adding the following line in the file log4j.properties:   {code}  log4j.category.com.atlassian.confluence.security.login.DefaultLoginManager=DEBUG  {code}  The following example trace will be added to the log file atlassian-confluence.log:   """
comments: "Team,     We have a scenario that we need to capture confluence login & logout info for audit and data governance purposes. This is seemless for Jira, however confluence doesn't provide a way to read login information from access log.    The workaround could be more useful if it prints additional info such as IP address, <USER>along with login time and user id. however for logout again we need to rely on access logs, this adds another complexity of getting logout and login info from two different files.    It will be useful to all the customers who want to audit the login/logout info.  Please reconsider this feature."

summary: "Built-in Interactive Search instead of Pagination"
description: """*Background*  Atlassian Applications such as Jira and Confluence implements _pagination_ as a user interface design feature. The purpose behind pagination is also to improve on Performance whereby loading of items are limited.    *Suggestion*  Most modern graphical user interfaces are using _Built-in Interactive Search_ which can be a better way instead of _pagination_. A search function would be able to return accurate results quicker instead of having the person to click on pages based on """"guesses"""" on where the items are.     If _pagination_ is still the way to go, then at least implement a global setting which defines the maximum number of entries one sees per page.    P.S: This might be a good source of reading, https://www.nngroup.com/articles/item-list-view-all/  """

summary: "Bundle Central License Availability plugin with Confluence"
description: """Confluence currently doesn't have an intuitive way to work with Licensing and Licensed User seats. See this feature request for background on this topic:  https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-54271    Atlassian has since released a plugin to help administrators find which users are counting towards a license called the *Central License Availability* plugin: [https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1220745/centralized-license-visibility]    And while the plugin doesn't ultimately solve the UI request (Crowd DC is required to take advantage of that feature), it does remove hurdles for admins who need to pull lists of active users counting towards a license with all types of implentations.     This [popular KB|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-get-a-list-of-active-users-counting-towards-the-confluence-license-298978076.html] can be used in some situations, however it has major caveats with respect to LDAP nested groups and duplicate users across directories. Both of these caveats can be safely ignored when leveraging the Central Visibility plugin. As a result, we would request that the plugin is bundled with Confluence files moving forward to aid in license management. """

summary: "Full Compatibility with JAWS "
description: """Currently visually impaired users who are using JAWS V2018.1808.10 (downloadable from www.freedomscientific.com) with Confluence are having issues where the Page Tree nodes are not easily distinguishable between the expand and collapse states. Currently JAWS  only says """"link"""" when referring to this link. This is currently affecting users productivity as it is difficult to understand the structure of a space. """
comments: "Hello dear Atlassian Team,    I am sad to see that the whole topic ""accessibility"" handled.    There should not be any required voting at all for somethng like ""accessibility"". We are talking about a requirement for handicapped people and not feature rquests.  The worldwide understanding of a software being accessible is, that this should be possible with basically any screen reader software. Especially in governments / public sector of countries,  we have the responsibility to ensure that such access is ensured. Not doing so would actually mean, having to deinstall a software.   See the comment from this customer too: https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFCLOUD-33573    I really have interest in seeing Atlassian taking screen reader compatibility more serious in 2020. The bug policy regarding accessibility issues should be reconsidered. If you get reported of  issues, there should not be any vote nessesary to do so. Help those people being able to enjoy your products. Proactive. In our case we are using sompany

summary: "Customization of the URL link in the space logo"
description: """Please, consider adding the possibility of customizing to where the URL of the confluence icon (that is on the top right corner of my space) sends the user when clicking on it, making it able to send the user to an external page.     If possible, it would also be helpful to be able to customize it according to which space is being used.    In my company we have three scenarios where that feature would be useful:   # Using confluence to display only pages, it would be useful to implement it with our site without disturbing the design. We could simply link directly to specific pages without being limitation and when the user click on the top right icon, he would be sent back to our site, basically having a better user experience (in that scenario). As it is now, i need to create a link on a page to achieve the same result.   # Im creating a space for our documentation in English and another in Portuguese. If i set my icon to send the user to the English space as the standard, when a user is reading the Portuguese version and clicks on our icon, he will be redirected to our English page, if that was contextual, it would be very practical.   # Considering the previous scenario, if i was able to work with external links, i would be able to resolve my problem simply trought the site side, not trough confluence.    Thanks in advance."""

summary: "Send in-app notifications for security vulnerabilities"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  When security vulnerabilities occur, Atlassian sends emails to the primary technical contacts associated with a license. Emails can be lost in email filters or spam boxes, which can leave administrators unaware of a vulnerability.     h3. Suggested Solution  Provide an in-app notification to Confluence administrators to make them aware of any vulnerabilities (in addition to sending emails).     h3. Why this is important   It is important that administrators be informed of vulnerabilities as soon as possible. By adding this feature, we will provide an additional channel through which an administrator can learn about vulnerabilities.     """

summary: "Confluence should not hold a database connection when it is not needed"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  On a super high-level description, Confluence occupies a database connection from the connection pool throughout the entirety of the HTTP request. Once that HTTP request returns, the database connection is returned to the pool.  This is a very complex topic on how Confluence handles database connections. Several enhancements and optimizations were done throughout the years to make sure we don't hold up a database connection for long. However, the first statement still holds.   When an HTTP request is required to interact with an external system, that database connection will still be held until a response is received from the remote server. In the case where you have a slowness with getting the response, the new incoming HTTP requests will open more database connections in Confluence.    h3. Suggested Solution  It would be nice to have Confluence use a database connection only when it is needed within the lifespan of an HTTP request.    h3. Workaround  Unfortunately, there are no workarounds at the moment."""

summary: "Require 2FA Challenge for Designated Spaces"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently Two Factor Authentication (2FA) can be enabled at an account level, requiring a user to use a secondary authenticator when logging in.    It would be useful to be able to prompt for 2FA when a user attempts to access particular spaces in order to ensure that the user who authenticated is still in control of the session.    This is a common function in many systems in order to access more sensitive information.    h3. Suggested Solution  Implement Space level 2FA for users who are already authenticated.    This 2FA should allow access to the resources with a controllable timeout.    h3. Workaround  None currently."""

summary: "Transfer Content Ownership"
description: """h4. Suggestion Summary  As an administrator, if I need to create a new username for one of my users for any given reasons, I would like to have an option in the UI to transfer content ownership from his/her old username to the new username.    h4. Workaround  We can run SQL Queries to make it happen, but, data manipulation is always risky. Knowledge Base Article with the workaround can be found [here|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-transfer-content-ownership-in-between-users-958450595.html]."""
comments: "We need this just for General scalability. We Farm out our space creation to IT support staff, so every single space has the wrong content owner on it from go. We need to be able to add a separate owner to the space and page (and create spaces on someone else's behalf.). For audit purposes, we also need to see the audit trail of ownership changes (when ownership changes for any reason) so database hackery is not really an option.    Also, the subject matter expert for pages often changes as people move to other projects and someone else takes over the old SME's content. This is fairly common. We also have old pages that need a proper owner (created by people no longer at the company, <USER> and we need the ability to click ""assign/take ownership"" so people can do that.    I tried Comala Workflows for this but the UX is too confusing (to the user) no matter how we work it, to be a satisfactory solution. Comala support helped me a lot with authoring the workflows to be the most intuitive/powerful, but in the

summary: "Don't force transition to Data Center with Data Center license"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    When admins upgrade to a data center license after their previous standalone license expires, applying the license to Confluence forces a migration to Data Center, whether the admin is ready to perform this transition or not.  h3. Suggested Solution    Allow the option to apply a Data Center license without migrating the instance to Data Center. This would allow admins to use a valid data center license on a standalone instance while taking time to plan for a transition to data center if needed.  h3. Workaround    If the previous license is expired, and the admin is not ready to transition to Data Center, the admin can generate a 30-day evaluation license at [https://my.<USER>https://my.<USER>] to buy time before the transition."""

summary: "Expand Notifications Capability for Confluence Questions"
description: """Currently, only watchers of a specific topic will be notified when a question is asked regarding that topic. Some admins may be keen to receive all notifications, even if they are not necessarily watchers for a given topic.     Some organizations could benefit from having a larger, configurable notification scheme to help drive adoption of Confluence Questions. Some examples would be:  * reminder notifications to admins when questions haven't been answered (configurable threshold from time of creation)   * questions with answers and no accepted answer (configurable threshold) """

summary: "Add email address as parameter for REST API"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently only username is a parameter for group updates and user information search.    h3. Suggested Solution  For getting user information or group updates, add email address as parameter."""
comments: "In our particular use case, there are times in automation where we want to get the username of the user given the user's email address using the res, <USER>rpc or other APIs. and then use that username with other APIs.    There seems to be no way to do this in Confluence server as the existing APIs either public and private only seem to take username.    By contrast Jira has APIs that can use either email address or username to look up a user.               "

summary: "Ability to display dynamic list of space names for every user without giving access to their contents"
description: """h3. Problem  Currently, other users who do not have access to restricted spaces cannot see if a space with a particular name exists.     h3. Suggested Solution  Ability for admins to create _Spaces_ name list that also gets updated dynamically for all users to see.    h3. Why This Is Important  This feature would help managers for intra/inter-departmental collaboration. One such scenario is when they have new spaces created but have a bit of trouble keeping track of what's available, so being able to see and request space access based on their names would be helpful.    h3. Workaround  Admins can create a list from the database and attach it to a Confluence page but it needs manual intervention to keep it up-to-date:    """

summary: "Ability to compress images in Confluence"
description: """Some users are experiencing performance issues with their Confluence instance, especially due to the latency transfering content from the server to the clients.     What would be useful to avoid performance issue is an option to have Confluence do the compression for an the image that is being uploaded.      As mentioned in CONFSERVER-23720  {quote}An analogous situation would be how a smart phone will reduce the resolution of a picture before being sent in a text message. Below are potential features we would like to see.    -The option to set a maximum KB size for images and automatically edit images to fit this requirement upon upload   -The option to have the compressed image displayed on the page but still have the original image available for download    The ultimate goal being to reduce the complexity of making sure images on pages do not slow the Confluence end user experience.  {quote}  Another customer input in the previous ticket that is valid for this situation :  {quote}Most of our confluence users are in developing countries with very low bandwith.   Large images slow the loading of pages significantly and sometimes pages can't be even loaded or there is a timeout.    An automatic compression can move us forward...   We have tried to approach the users informing and instructing to upload smaller images but that represents more work and it's just easier to drag and drop.
