The result: hundreds of pages with very large photo galleries that can be only loaded in a couple of countries, instead of the 33 in Asia, Africa and Latin America.  {quote}"""
comments: "All modern content management systems provide image optimization as a standard service offering. At Airbnb we are experiencing adoption issues in countries where bandwidth is a concern. Would love some advice on plugins or this feature request becoming part of a future enhancement."

summary: "Ability to configure a limit on the number of Space Administrators by Space on Confluence"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Current Space Administrators are free to add the [Space Admin permission|https://confluence.<USER>doc/space-permissions-overview-139521.html] to any user or group, without any configurable limit on the number of space administrators.    h3. Suggested Solution  As a Confluence administrator, I would like to globally limit the number of space administrators on each Space.  When a Space admin is adding the space admin permission to new users or groups and if that limit is surpassed, then the admin would receive a friendly notification saying no more admins could be added due to the limit.  This functionality must be able to check the number of users under a group (consider nested groups as well).    h3. Why this is important  Limiting the maximum number of space administrators on all spaces would help to avoid current administrators adding large security groups, without knowing who's part of that group.  As Uncle <USER>said, _with great power comes great responsibility_; and great is the power assigned to a Space administrator, even being able to delete the full space.  Limiting the number of space admins by space is a way to safeguard content, insuring this permissions is wisely assigned.     h3. Workaround  Periodically review the administrators of each space and perform manual _curation_ of space admins.  The following KB articles may help the Confluence administrator to determine the users and groups with Space admin permissions:   * [How do I view a list of all space administrators for all spaces|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-do-i-view-a-list-of-all-space-administrators-for-all-spaces-346325879.html]   * [How to list which spaces a group can access|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-list-which-spaces-a-group-can-access-720636535.html]   * [How to Check Permissions for a Space via SQL Queries|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-check-permissions-for-a-space-via-sql-queries-717062216.html]"""

summary: "Ability to Toggle Version Information on Confluence Pages On/Off"
description: """h2. Suggestion Summary  Confluence should provide administrators with a way to toggle displaying version related information on/off.    h2. Suggestion Details  If unauthenticated, one can access Confluence's landing page and retrieve version related information from three places:    * Login page footer.  * Response Head AJS Tags.  * Response Body What's New Link.    Someone without good intentions could use this information to search for possible security vulnerabilities over the internet and hack Confluence. Therefore, if in a public facing instance, hiding these pieces of information can become a requirement for administrators.    h2. Workaround  An administrator can modify Confluence core files to prevent this information from being displayed. The knowledge base below teaches how to do that:  * [How to Hide Version Information on Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-hide-version-information-on-confluence-980460833.html]"""
comments: "I agree with 'Someone without good intentions could use this information to search for possible security vulnerabilities over the internet and hack Confluence'         It was our immediate thought after we saw the version of Confluence plastered all over the website and any notifications. It would be good to just be able to toggle a switch or setting to stop displaying the version you are using from any notification or splash screen instead of editing all the different xml files to accomplish this." [SEP] "We recently performed a Hacking simulation on our Corporate Site and received similar feedback on the risks from sharing Confluence version number in plain view.     Obviously, we rather not customise our installation, to allow smooth and quick upgrades. Introducing this ""Toggle switch"" would allow this flexibility to System Admins to resolve this risk and in our case satisfy our IT Security team.         Please take this into consideration and pick up this suggestion to next versions scope.     Rega

summary: "Shadow information"
description: """Hi,    I'm using Jira/Confluence professionally as well as at home and like them more and more. There are a few things I would really have great use for.    * Shadowing information.  For example when writing How-to's, and you describe similar processes, you either have to create a link, or write the information again. It would be very good if i were possible to just shadow/mirror the information in-place.     If having many such cases, (how-to's can be very long some times) it is not pleasing to having to open a lot of links, nor writing same stuff over and over.    As I don't know how this could/should be implemented if that is decided, I did not know what component to select below - just took a guess, please correct if terribly wrong.         Thanks"""

summary: "Restrict the space categorize feature to system admin only"
description: """h3. Description  Currently, system admin and space admin is able to set the category of space and also remove the category from the space. The customer would like to have the feature that restricts the space category feature for system admin only. They do not want the space admin to be able to add, edit or remove the space category for space.    h3. Suggestion  Provide an option to disable the space category option for non-system admin"""

summary: "Adding SAXParserFactory lookup under the system properties"
description: """As a part of Confluence Crowd integration so as to unmarshal the response from Crowd, we are using SAXParser. Now the problem is that during this process, we create createSAXParserFactory and tries to the load the implementation class which as per specification as below(snippet from https://docs.oracle.com/javase/8/docs/api/javax/xml/parsers/SAXParserFactory.html#newInstance-- )    ---------  Obtain a new instance of a SAXParserFactory. This static method creates a new factory instance This method uses the following ordered lookup procedure to determine the SAXParserFactory implementation class to load:  * Use the javax.xml.parsers.SAXParserFactory system property.  * Use the properties file """"lib/jaxp.properties"""" in the JRE directory. This configuration file is in standard java.util.Properties format and contains the fully qualified name of the implementation class with the key being the system property defined above. The jaxp.properties file is read only once by the JAXP implementation and it's values are then cached for future use. If the file does not exist when the first attempt is made to read from it, no further attempts are made to check for its existence. It is not possible to change the value of any property in jaxp.properties after it has been read for the first time.  * Use the service-provider loading facilities, defined by the ServiceLoader class, to attempt to locate and load an implementation of the service using the default loading mechanism: the service-provider loading facility will use the current thread's context class loader to attempt to load the service. If the context class loader is null, the system class loader will be used.  * Otherwise the system-default implementation is returned.  Once an application has obtained a reference to a SAXParserFactory it can use the factory to configure and obtain parser instances.      *Suggestion*  Lots of threads are blocked(snippet below) on finding the specific implementation and hence probably it might help if we go ahead and add below system property for a better performance around this lookup  -Djavax.xml.parsers.SAXParserFactory=org.apache.xerces.jaxp.SAXParserFactoryImpl           """

summary: "Allow only view permissions for Anonymous Access"
description: """As of now, Confluence does not have a feature to allow Confluence Administrator to restrict the options of permissions that Space Admin could grant towards the users/anonymous access.     For example, when a Confluence Administrator wants Space Admin to only have the ability to control (enable/disable) the {{view}} permission of a space. It is not achievable right now as there exist no configurations for Confluence Administrator to restrict the permissions that Space Admin owns."""

summary: "Confluence asynchronous logging "
description: """Request that Confluence be architected to use asynchronous logging for enhanced performance.     Currently Confluence 6.x uses Log4j-1 which has AsyncAppender, however we are not programmatically implementing this feature.     [Log4j-2|https://logging.apache.org/log4j/2.x/] supports asynchronous logging (see the """"Improved Performance section), requesting that Log4j-2 or above be considered to be used in future versions of Confluence and Confluence implement this functionality. """

summary: "Add ability to automate the Confluence function assessment"
description: """h3. Problem    Some customers would like to automate the Confluence function assessment when testing on the new Confluence releases. It's better to have a report as a result. The list of the functionalities (not limited to)   * Editor (page editing, collaborative editing)   * Page creation   * Space creation   * Permissions   * Attachments   * Export page   * Search    h3. Workaround   * [Confluence REST API|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/confluence-server-rest-api/]  * [Confluence REST API examples|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/confluence-rest-api-examples/]"""

summary: "Can we use the same source of documents for many spaces?"
description: """Hi,    Now we would like to design document system in software company. We would like to organize documents according to belows criterias:   # Project categories   # Work areas such as; BA, DEV, QA, Other categories   # Department categories.    # The same documents are not duplicates in above categories. If we create many spaces for projects and teams, many same documents are dupicated to create, store, maintain.     So, which best solution should we do in this case?    Br,    Nhung"""

summary: "Bulk change ownership during XML Space Import"
description: """As a +system admin+    I want to +have a way to change pages owners (author, last modifier) during a Space import operation+   So that +If I receive an exported space in XML format, I can easily do a """"from-to"""" operation to assign pages to new owners+  h4. Suggestion Summary   - Customer company A is bought by company B.   - Both have Confluence, Directories are not integrated   - Sysadmins on company B create users for employees from company A, but they do not always match the ones they had in A (such as, A is johnsmith, B is jsmith)   - Customer would rather have pages assigned correctly, than listing _unknown user johnsmith_    h4. Proposed Solution    Import operation could allow admins to input a from-to CSV file indicating old user names and new ones, to prevent that issue.  h4. Possible Workarounds   - Have the space imported in a non-production instance and run the procedures from:   [How To - Transfer Content Ownership in between Users|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-transfer-content-ownership-in-between-users-958450595.html]   - Try find-and-replace operations on the XML file such as        Again, we must be mindful about the space name. I don't think you gonna have spaces with usernames on it, but, just in case, be mindful about that. If that happens, after you have SED'ed your way, open the entities.xml file and look for the tag below:    Once you found it, just replace the name accordingly to respect the previous space name and save the file. Then follow the steps previously outlined here and we should be fine.  """

summary: "As a Confluence Administrator I should be able to enable a System App (or a module) from the UPM UI if it is currently disabled"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Sometimes a System App, or a module of a System App, might be disabled while Confluence is running or during startup. This could happen by a bug on Confluence or modified directly from the database.   A Confluence administrator isn't able to enable this App/Module from the Universal Plugin Manager UI, requiring some downtime to restart the application when the problem is identified.    Note that this enhancement is not targeted on being able to disable these Apps/modules, but solely on the ability to *enable* them.  h3. Suggested Solution    When a System App (or module of a system app) is disabled, give the Confluence Administrator the ability to enable it from the UPM UI.  h3. Workarounds  h4. Use UPM REST API  Call UPM REST API to change the status of a given plugin or a single module  * [UPM REST API|https://ecosystem.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/UPM/pages/6094960/UPM+REST+API]    h4. Directly updating the database  Enable the App or the module via data manipulation on the database.  Refer to the following KB articles on how to accomplish it:   * [How to Manually Change a Plugin Back to Default State Through the Database|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-manually-change-a-plugin-back-to-default-state-through-the-database-592609333.html]   * [How to reset all Confluence plugins back to their default state through the database|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-reset-all-confluence-plugins-back-to-their-default-state-through-the-database-859509873.html]   * [How Do I find enabled and disabled plugins in the database?|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/how-do-i-find-enabled-and-disabled-plugins-in-the-database/]"""

summary: "As a Confluence Space administrator I would like to be able to create public pages within my space"
description: """When managing a large space/ organization it would be a good feature to be able to share viewing rights of pages with people outside of the permission scheme of the page tree / space.     The way it is currently done within our organization is to set up two top-level areas. One public and one private within the same space. This approach means a lot of copy-pasting between the two areas to keep them in sync, which creates a lot of manual work for space administrators."""
comments: "Hi [~<USER>    Thanks for raising this issue. The description here is quite similar to the following suggestions that we already have:   * CONFSERVER-37884 Ability to grant higher permissions for pages than their parent permission (restrict <-> grant)   * CONFSERVER-7089 create the ability to add page permissions for users and groups    Please check if the suggestions above are what we're looking for. Feel free to ""vote"" and watch for those issues instead, so that we'll be able to accurately track the interest of this feature.    Thanks!   Monique - Atlassian Support"

summary: "Allow Admins to influence search results through a mechanism available in the UI "
description: """Currently, Confluence has a very specific algorithm that it considers when returning Confluence Search Results. Sometimes, these results are not what the admin would expect OR are not as relevant to the admin.     While we do have additional search syntax/refinements that can be leveraged to yield more relevant results to the user, it would be ideal if the admin themselves could assign value to certain assets such that they are surfaced more easily through a basic keyword search. """

summary: "Feature improvement to File conversion service"
description: """In reviewing [File conversion service causes out of memory errors in Confluence 5.7 or above|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/file-conversion-service-causes-out-of-memory-errors-in-confluence-5-7-or-above-703398523.html] the suggestions that were provided are not the most ideal situations in handling what essentially is a situation where there could be an unbounded memory utilization that will take out the entire application.  h2. Documented Suggestions and There Issues    The suggestion to increase the heap increases assume that we know what the max amount of memory is going to be needed to complete the operation, which we don't. Therefor, it becomes a game of chicken. Also requires the reservation of large amounts of memory that probably wont actually be used.    Disabling the file conversion service is not really a good choice, as it makes working in the UI awkward as panels show but with no content, thus making the implementation look broken.    The last suggestion for Data Center to reroute the file conversion traffic essentially is saying to take a node and dedicate it to just this operation, but it still doesn't solve the issue of the node/instance going out when a large file is hit. It also introduces configuration complexity and yet another thing that can go wrong to have to handle that makes the overall product look unstable.  h2. Proposed Solution    As this has to do with a situation of a potentially unbounded memory need event, much like you have already accomplished with the Synchrony service, this service should be pushed off to an independent process that is controlled by the Confluence parent process. Thus one could monitor the process for health, re-configure it 'on the fly', restart it, and have it do the potentially hazardous work without putting the Confluence Core process at risk. There are a number of 3rd party plugins that have worked out how to move 'high risk' work off to child processes so that they don't put the 'prime' instance at risk also. This should also help with the perceived overall stability of the app as having to restart it randomly doesn't help."""

summary: "Related Content Macro"
description: """Hello,     I've been using a number of your label and other macros in Confluence, but have just not found the right fit for what I'm looking for in our knowledgebase. On each of our main pages, I'd like to include what I'm calling a """"Related Content"""" macro. Which would essentially take the labels on the current page and show you any number of pages you specify of content also using those labels. I would also love the option to include labels from child pages and to indicate if I want related content shown only from the current space, or all spaces associated with our company/account. The Content by Label macro is inferior for this because as content is added or labels change, you have to manually go into each macro and add or delete labels. There is also no control over the space it uses to do this. It seems like this would avoid the issues around the Most Popular Content/Labels and indexing.    This would be more work, but the other way to look at this would essentially be a knowledgebase search based on either labels or words on the page and restricted to certain spaces with a limit on how many links were provided, but automatically displayed at the bottom of each page that includes this macro. So a generic or """"related"""" search is unnecessary and reduces the time it takes to find content.    Happy to answer questions.     """

summary: "Add extended support for CloudFormation templates for other DB types"
description: """Customers have reported that they would like to use something other than RDS / Aurora / Postgres for their databases due to their organization infrastructure setup. If they wanted to take advantage of Auto-Scaling Groups or other components that we provide in the Atlassian-AWS CloudFormation Templates, then they would need to extensively rework them for their own needs.    The suggestion serves as support for adding different database types for CloudFormation templates aside from what we currently offer."""

summary: "REST API Endpoint for Content Indexing Queue pending items"
description: """For monitoring purposes, it would be helpful to have an endpoint that would return the output of the page http://<confluence>/admin/viewindexqueue.action, focusing the output to the amount of pending indexing items in the queue.    That'd help customers have visibility of their index queue in a proactive way, having them take measures when that queue is increasing and not decreasing a given period of time."""
comments: "We have a very large instance of Confluence Data Center.  Recently, we ran into an issue with a scheduled job that flooded our content indexing queue causing it to back-up.  This in turn caused our performance to degrade and end-users were not able to find information in Confluence Search because the content had not been indexed.     Issues reported by the end-users prompted us to investigate and then we discovered the content indexing was backed up.    This caused to do a full re-build of our index.    If we would have received an earlier warning we could have proactively addressed the issue and prevented rebuilding the entire index."

summary: "Customize Global and Space Activity"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    * Currently, the Global and Space Activity of Confluence shows pre-defined information about an instance, without the possibility of customizing it.    h3. Suggested Solution    * Add the ability for our customers to customize what information they would like to have displayed in their Global and Space Activity.    h3. Why this is important    * Each company has its own necessary information for creating reports and auditing purposes. Some information shown in the Global and Space Activity might not be very relevant to display, so customizing this area would reduce the unnecessary content shown."""
comments: "This is a very important and useful feature. Please release it soon."

summary: "Reword ""Write a comment..."" hint"
description: """Some of our users keep clicking the generic comment box at the bottom, when they actually try to reply to an existing comment - even if they are trying to respond to a nested existing comment.    While I can agree that we can blame those users, I still think it is not productive to make them feel bad... especially since this will not solve the issue for other new users who will have the same problem until corrected.    Therefore, I believe a different wording would probably help the users at least pause before clicking, for example something like """"Start a new comment thread..."""" or whatever else you guys may deem more suitable.    Alternatively, this could maybe be changed into a button with wording that clearly informs the user that it will be a whole new comment thread, and not a correct way to reply to an existing comment?"""

summary: "Additional Synchrony logging "
description: """As a Confluence administrator, it would be helpful to have improved logging round Synchrony:    * In a scenario where Confluence Data Center is managing Synchrony, and the internal proxy is not enabled, Sychrony traffic will be directed through /synchrony first, and failing that, will try /synchrony-proxy and finally confluence-context-path/synchrony-proxy. It wold be useful to know when/why Confluence uses the alternate paths to reach Synchrony.   * Similarly, logging around failed websocket upgrades could help administrators understand why the failover to XHR occurs.  * There's a lot of noise in the Synchrony logs, and so the ability to filter out the noise would be useful. Unfortunately, changing the level of Synchrony logging from DEBUG to WARN (for example) stops all logging, so it can't be done that way.   """
comments: "Hi. Synchrony has logging on various levels, and default (at least in the dev configuration) is INFO, which is not as noisy as DEBUG, but most certainly logs various things. How do you try to change synchrony logging level? I'll take a look at what sets synchrony logging level to DEBUG." [SEP] "Documentation has been updated to include some information on how to change the logging level in both managed and standalone Synchrony, and how to strip the DEBUG prefix from the managed synchrony logs.      See https://confluence.<USER>display/DOC/Configuring+Synchrony"

summary: "Create a Jira macro in Confluence that displays which users are assigned to a Jira project's role"
description: """h3. Problem Definition      * Some users manage their teams and their roles on Confluence and would like to have a list of which users are assigned to a specific role in Jira, on each project.    h3. Suggested Solution      * Create a macro that lists the roles of a Jira project and which users are assigned to each role.    h3. Why this is important    * This makes it easier to manage which users should have a specific role on each project."""

summary: "Adding attachments to a page via REST API does not append to existing e-mail thread"
description: """I'm using Confluence 6.15.2 but this does not seem to have been fixed as of 7.0.2: [https://confluence.<USER>doc/confluence-7-0-release-notes-973479448.html].    Adding new attachments to a page creates a new e-mail thread instead of using the e-mail thread generated with create content (POST /rest/api/content). When adding new attachments is done manually, it correctly concatenates to one thread.    This functionality seems to be on Confluence's side: [https://confluence.<USER>conf615/email-notifications-967338126.html].    """"*Email threading*: Confluence will attempt to group all the email notifications about changes to a specific page together.""""    !image-2019-10-24-17-13-08-798.png|width=598,height=47!"""

summary: "Confluence 7.0 EAP feedback wrt. effects of CDN integration on content exports"
description: """This has been discussed with <USER>from Atlassian here: [https://community.developer.<USER>t/confluence-7-0-eap-released/28060/33]  {quote}The internal Confluence PDF exporter injects a webResourceIntegration bean which has an API to query the state of the CDN system (WebResourceIntegration#getCDNStrategy) and remove that from the rendered DOM.   Avoiding fetching from the CDN on PDF exports is a precaution done for several reasons:    Some site admins might be reluctant to open up communications from the Confluence instance to the CDN (without this, only one way traffic is needed).   The way the internal PDF exporter works with web resources, there may be scenarios where such a request could poison the CDN cache with resources that we don't want to make their way to a user who views that page, for example.   Adding to the whitelist is an option we're considering but it will not solve the above difficulties.    I'd be happy to discuss the technical details of this but it would be better to do it in a ticket.   ...  {quote}  All of our exporters render content in the PDF output mode - regardless of the actual filetype (Word, PDF, ...) like this:    Since the built-in PDF exporter also needs to handle the CDN links specifically I was wondering if it wouldn't make sense to simply disable CDN integration when rendering content in this or related output modes. Another option could be a property in the conversion context or a thread local.    What do you think?    Thanks!"""
comments: "Heya [~<EMAIL>],    The current surefire solution that I can recommend (and the one that we went with internally) is to fetch the CDN URL using the injectable:    and querying the CDN strategy using:    This can return null (for no CDN) or a PrefixCDNStrategy which you can use to retrieve the CDN URL prefix.         The web resource manager (or WRM) is a complex and stateful system which, at the moment, doesn't allow us to easily flick CDN support on and off via a ThreadLocal or similar.    Adding the CDN URL to the whitelist is a sensible thing to do and is definitely on our backlog.         Can you confirm that the above solves your problem, even if not in the most ""elegant"" way?" [SEP] "Hi [~<USER>, sorry for the late response.    Concerning your question: We should probably be able to solve it that way although it will most probably require some rather dirty reflection hacks. Since we still have to support older versions of Confluence (currently 6.0), we can't compile against Confluence 7 and use the

summary: "As an administrator I would like to disable all 3rd Party Apps on startup of a Confluence Data Center"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Disabling 3rd party apps on startup is a great functionality to troubleshoot issues caused by installed Apps (aka Plugins, Addons).    This functionality is available in the Server deployment by using the {{--disable-all-addons}} attribute as described in [this KB|https://confluence.<USER>doc/start-and-stop-confluence-838416264.html].    If we try to use the same functionality in a Data Center instance it won't work and we get the following message in {{atlassian-confluence.log}}:    h3. Suggested Solution    Since Apps' state is mapped in the database, Confluence could check if the cluster is being initiated (one node only running) and accept the option to disable 3rd Party Apps.  h3. Workaround    As workaround, you may disable each App from the database following the instructions in [this KB|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-manually-remove-malfunctioning-add-ons-113705118.html]."""

summary: "Add Support for Aurora Serverless Databases on AWS"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *Confluence Server* including *Confluence Data Center* and only specifically Aurora Serverless. Are you looking for Aurora non-serverless? [See the corresponding suggestion|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-36248].    {panel}    Amazon has developed a MySQL compatible serverless database server, [Aurora|http://aws.amazon.com/rds/aurora/], that is available for use as part of AWS. As there are many instances of Confluence installed in AWS, Aurora serverless support should be added into Confluence. According to Amazon information, Aurora is designed to be compatible with MySQL and postgresql, so that existing applications and tools can run without requiring modification."""

summary: "Team Calendars should take space edit permissions into account"
description: """Currently anyone that can view a space can perform actions on the calendar view of that space including:   * Adding a new calendar   * Importing an existing calendar    It seems problematic that if a user can only view content in a space, that they are able to modify the calendar view. On the other hand, there is no specific """"add/edit calendar"""" permission in a space like there is for pages, blogs, comments and attachments. So it is unclear how this will work. One suggestion might be that only space administrators could add or import calendars but that is changing how team calendars works. Another might be that it should follow the """"add/edit pages"""" permission.    In addition to that, users can currently create calendars and associate those calendars with spaces that they may only have view permissions on. Again, a suggestion might be to only allow this for space administrators as there is no """"add/edit calendar"""" permission in a space. Or perhaps it should check against the """"add/edit pages"""" permission instead."""
comments: "Users created too much calendars in one space, please improve this function ASAP:("

summary: "Provide the Cache Statistics page as a Log File"
description: """Currently the only way to review the current cache performance in both Confluence Server and Data Center is to visit the cache statistics page as an admin via the UI:    *<Confluence-Base-Url>/admin/cache/showStatistics.action#fullView*    It would be extremely convenient if there was a log file with this same information provided somewhere in the environment so that an admin could pull the information with ease without having to access the UI and be able to run quick comparisons among the log files. """
comments: "Since this is partly generated for me, there are a couple of things that would give some additional insight so some sort of analysis can take place:    1) Provide some information on the distributed cache items. Giving an effectiveness of ""Unknown"" is really unhelpful. If it can't be calculated, how can one determine how to adjust it?    2) When its distributed, the hits/misses/evicted are generally unknown as well (just question marks) Again, If it can't be calculated, how can one determine how to adjust it? Way too many question marks in evicted.    3) In Ehcache (I'm DC so I have the Hazelcast caching, according to documentation) the cumulative values were a lot more helpful, as I could wait until Wednesday (we restart every Sunday morning) and take a snapshot of the values, pop it into a spreadsheet and compare values from week to week (nice little accident that you can select the page table content and paste it into a spreadsheet). This allowed me to adjust the values a little at a time to help custo

summary: "Page Restrictions based on additional factors (time, date, IP, etc.)"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently page restrictions can be set manually and this controls who has access to view and edit the page.    Users would like more control over this, such as determining when it's possible to modify a page.    h3. Suggested Solution  Provide ability to only allow edits during certain times, date ranges, IP address (current location), <USER>    The IP address idea would ensure that you can only edit a page if you're within a specific network, for example.    h3. Why this is important   Greater control of Confluence """
comments: "Another use case for this feature would be enforcing deadlines to confluence pages.    For example, release notes need to be finalized by 6pm so you would schedule the page to be locked at 6 pm. "

summary: "As an Admin I'd like to be able to move move a Space Template to the Global Template list"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Currently Space Templates are available in a specific space only and sometimes     h3. Suggested Solution    Confluence could have an option to move a Space Template, or even all Space Templates for an specific Space, to the Global Templates list.    h3. Why this is important    Creating a template can be very time consuming and sometimes Space Templates might be needed for other Spaces, either because it was created as a Space template by mistake or maybe due to a business decision that template is needed in other Spaces.     It would be very important if Admins had an option to *easily* move a Space Template to the Global Template list.    h3. Workaround    It is possible to simply port over the template content manually into a new global template using the Confluence Source Editor. As mentioned in our [How to migrate a space template to a global template|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-migrate-a-space-template-to-a-global-template-966038022.html] KB."""

summary: "Ability to generate API token"
description: """Problem Definition  In Cloud, it is possible to create an API token for authentication. This is not possible in Confluence Server.    Suggested Solution  Provide an ability to generate a REST API token for Confluence Server    """
comments: "Without this feature, the user is essentially forced to:   # Introduce significant security risks by having to   ## Use saved (clear) username/passwords   ## Creating a ""special system"" (dummy) user   # Increase cost by reducing integration options and creating specialist integration points     ## Requiring an ""OAuth-compliant web application"" <-- i.e. not using Excel or a script    ## Writing a fully fledged plugin   # Migrating your data and paying for JIRA Server   ## This feels like a forced option and is in danger of being perceived as manipulation of buying choices by deliberate user frustration" [SEP] "Hello,    While ticket in _Gathering Interest_ status you can use my plugin [API Tokens for Confluence|https://marketplace.<USER>apps/1221350/api-tokens-for-confluence?hosting=server&tab=overview&utm_source=jira]  Would love to hear any feedback on it :)  " [SEP] "I'm a little surprised this hasn't been implemented yet, especially because it's been supported by the Cloud version for a long time. B

summary: "Disable Watch functionality per Space including email notifications of that Space"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  As usage grows, Confluence can end up sending a lot more notifications than desired due to page or space level watches.    Currently, a Confluence/Space Administrator needs to manually remove watchers at the page and space level one-by-one via View Page > Manage Watchers. This can be cumbersome if there are a lot of pages and individual watchers. Additionally, there is no way to turn off notifications for a given space in general.    h3. Suggested Solution  The suggestion is to have the following capabilities in a configuration page:  # As an admin, disable watching an entire Space in bulk (for individual page watches, space watches, or both)  # Turn off email notifications (or, prevent watching) for a specific Space entirely"""

summary: "As a Confluence user I'd like to have options to create reports with information from the Jira projects"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    There are very limited options when trying to create a report in Confluence based on Jira project's information.       h3. Suggested Solution    It would be great to have a more dynamic macro in Confluence to integrate with Jira to create reports on releases for example.  In the example below, customer is trying to use Page Properties Macro and Page Properties Report Macro to try to achieve this goal:    Currently we see a very static information like:   !report_macro.png|thumbnail!    But it would be great if we had the option to group releases under specific projects, and have an option to count releases and other data for example.   !Report.png|thumbnail!      h3. Why this is important    At this point the options available are paid third-party plugins and having this new feature would be adding a great value to the Confluence - Jira integration.    h3. Workaround    Use third-party plugins to achieve this."""

summary: "Poll or survey capability"
description: """Thanks for looking at my suggestion.    I suggest you add a poll or survey capability to Confluence without needing a plug-in.  Polling is a basic, basic component of a collaboration suite – which is the basic premise of using Confluence – and that _should_ be part of the regular package.    Plug-ins are great, but they can shift around and  require IT/management/<USER> involvement.    Polls should be there if Confluence wants to claim being a top-tier collaboration tool."""

summary: "Read-only administrator permission"
description: """h3. Problem Definition   * Currently, there isn't an option to give a user access to the General Configuration screen or the Space Tools unless the user is a Confluence administrator or a space administrator.    h3. Suggested Solution   * Create a middle permission role, where a user can access this information available on the Confluence's administration screen (as well as the space's administration area) but cannot edit any information of it.    h3. Why this is important   * This is important to companies who wish to give some users access to their instance's information for auditing purposes, without risking having these users edit any settings of the instance."""

summary: "Provide the functionality to perform bulk delete for unsynced users"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Starting from Confluence 6.13, admins can delete users that are unsynced from Confluence. Unsynced users are users that are not synced any more when an external user directory is being synchronized. This could be because the user has been removed from the external directory or it is because the user directory filters no longer include that user <USER>  While this feature is useful, the current implementation only allows the deletion of these users individually and there is no option to perform bulk delete.    h3. Suggested Solution  Having the bulk delete feature would greatly help."""
comments: "Hello,    As you are ""gathering interrest"" of this, this is one: because of GDPR laws in Europe, we are requested to delete personal information about people who left the company (this is why this ""unsync users"" feature has been introduced in Confluence 6.13.    As we are using Confluence since 6 years now, with a current population of 14.000 users, we have *hundreds* of people who left the company and we will now need to be removed. We can't humanely do it manually.    It's a good thing to have provided a way to keep the thing clean in the future, but you have to think about the past too.    In addition to that, if we would like to develop an automated process to cleanup people information in sync with their termination in the company, a REST API is needed.      " [SEP] "Please make it happen. This seems to be an easy enhancement.  Hao" [SEP] "Our instance has 18257 unsynced users atm.    What am i supposed to do? Run selenium scripts on it?" [SEP] "Well, I have around 90,000 unsynced users right now.

summary: "Customize ""All updates"" on the Dashboard by groups"
description: """There is an existing suggestion request to provide the [CONFSERVER-54284 - Ability to customize """"All updates"""" on the Dashboard|https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-54284]. It would be better if the dashboard the customization can customize differently for each group in Confluence.    For example, the feature should provide restrictions to content updates to a group. When a user that belongs to *group A* logs into Confluence, the user should only be able to view updates from *group A*."""

summary: "Add bulk search and replace"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently there is no way to bulk update content across multiple pages/spaces. An example scenario would be:  Imagine you create documentation for a bike. You've got 40 pages, in which you mention a helm 15 times. At some time, you decide to rename """"helm"""" to """"rudder"""". It would be great if there was a way to track the number of occurrences of “helm” and update all of them to “rudder”.  h3. Suggested Solution  Create a tool or macro to track those changes and allow admins to implement the search and replace when necessary.   h3. Why this is important  This is really helpful for technical documentation, since certain technical components change over time and it’s difficult to update the documentation in every location. """
comments: "Considering that huge content needs maintenance *without* potentially harming the database I strongly vote for this feature to be included. Extensions to this functionality could be:    * Limiting replacements to a certain page tree and manual selection of pages   * Limiting replacements by pages having a certain label   * Adding a preview of the content (excerpt)    * Renaming Page Titles as well as the certain words in there are often mentioned as part of the content inside "

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I would like to automatically create a Personal Space on user's first login"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Personal spaces can only be created when the user follows the steps in [Create your personal space|https://confluence.<USER>doc/create-your-personal-space-777010869.html].  Currently, there's no option to automatically create a Personal Space for every user in Confluence that has the _Personal Space_ permissions.    h3. Suggested Solution  If the user has the _Personal Space_ [global permission|https://confluence.<USER>doc/global-permissions-overview-138709.html], create a personal space on the first login.  It could even be an opt-out option on the on-boarding plugin.    h3. Workaround   The Confluence administrator can create the personal space of a user by running the [{{addPersonalSpace}} RPC API method|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/remote-confluence-methods/#management].  Below is an example of how to run that method using {{curl}}.    (!) Note that this RPC API method bypasses _Personal Space_ [global permission|https://confluence.<USER>doc/global-permissions-overview-138709.html], so it will create the space even if the target user doesn't have it.    With this, the admin can create an automation to add a Personal Space for new users after validating this user has the _Personal Space_ [global permission|https://confluence.<USER>doc/global-permissions-overview-138709.html]."""

summary: "Asynchronously render attachments when loading a Confluence page"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Files can be rendered in a Confluence page using different macros, such as the [{{view-file}}|https://confluence.<USER>doc/view-file-macro-170494360.html] or an internal link as described in [Display Files and Images|https://confluence.<USER>doc/display-files-and-images-245827130.html].    Rendering files within Confluence may be an expensive task specially depending on how many files a page needs to render. Confluence needs to run some tasks on the background before displaying the file in the UI.    Since these tasks are performed on the same thread that is loading the page, tasks associated to attachments may negatively contribute to the total time a page takes to load.    h3. Suggested Solution  If we could perform all the backend processes from these macros asynchronously to the page loading, then it could decrease the time it takes to load the page.  Note that this would not decrease the total time to load the entire page, but would at least load the content and release the browser to the user while more heavy loading is occurring on the backend.  This is already in place for the Jira Issues macro and something similar could be implemented here.     h3. Workaround   As a workaround, the [Future Macro|https://bobswift.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CACHE/pages/42631201/Future+Macro] could be used, where available, to asynchronously load heavy content.    As an example, loading a sample page with 50 {{view-file}} macros took _997 ms_ on a vanilla instance:        The same page took _412 ms_ to load when all the files are loaded from a single _Future macro_, giving the user a feeling the page is loading faster while another background job loads the files.  """

summary: "Allow Space admins to disable Global templates in Space level"
description: """h3. Issue summary  # Currently, when a template is enabled on the Global level, it will appear in all Spaces.  # A Space admin will be unable to disable the templates from appearing in their Spaces.  # It would be good to have the option to disable certain Global templates in the Space level.    h4. Current behavior  # Global template appears on all Spaces with the Space admins not having an option to disable them.    h4. Suggested behavior  # Space admins should have the option to disable Globally templates in their respective Spaces."""

summary: "Confluence stemming should be more lenient"
description: """Confluence currently uses the [K-stem algorithm|http://lexicalresearch.com/kstem-doc.txt] which can be quite restrictive in what it considers to be words that have the same stem. Some examples of words that one might expect to match but don't match are:   * Manage and Management   * Manage and Managers   * Manage and Managerial   * Memory and Memorial   * Memory and Memorize"""

summary: "Add sender restriction while sharing a page in Confluence"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  An organization typically has global distribution lists that are used to grant global access to spaces.  But, currently, every user part of this global distribution list is able to share pages in Confluence to that distribution list.    h3. Suggested Solution  It would be useful for admins to have the ability to restrict users (like a blacklist) to prevent them from sharing pages to the distribution list.    h3. Why this is important     This is to prevent certain users from accidentally sharing content to the whole organization.    h3. Workaround   (Optional)"""

summary: "Selecting pages for Custom Export of Space"
description: """In Space exports, Custom Export option can be used if pages need to be individually selected. Though, if the Space has many pages, individually selecting pages can be a pain.    h3. *Suggestion*    This process of selecting multiple pages can be improved in a couple of ways.  # If the selections of pages can be made once and then saved via a configuration file  #* This will allow users to upload configuration next time and export  # Confluence can remember last selected pages"""
comments: "I think this is a very important function. For my case, i use confluence as a knowledge depository, sometimes i have to print a certain set of articles over and over again. However, in confluence, that would mean that i have to reselect all the pages every time i need them and it could be up to 50 or more pages. This function can definitely help me shorten my work around time."

summary: "Improve the compatibility check for the Confluence Mobile app"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  In order to use the Confluence mobile app, administrators must make the {{<confluence-base-url>/server-info.action}} url accessible without authentication. More details can be found in ['Can't check compatibility' error in the Confluence Server mobile app|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/can-t-check-compatibility-error-in-the-confluence-server-mobile-app-943523933.html].    For some administrators who use Okta/SAML or have very secure network requirements, this is not possible.     h3. Suggested Solution  Create a way to have Confluence validate the Confluence version without having to allow unauthenticated requests to go to the server-info.action URL.    h3. Workaround   Currently there is no workaround. """

summary: "We need a linux version of Companion for Confluence"
description: """Your Companion application is a great tool for editing documents attached to pages in Confluence. However even though there is a significant number of users with Linux as their primary desktop OS you only provide Companion for Windows and MacOS.    There was a suggestion for a Linux version some time ago, https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-56399 but this is closed with a Won't fix status. The clossing message in the comments is upright ridicolous. It tells us you will not make a <USER>version and then suggest that we install Companion for editing douments, which is not available for <USER> Not very logical...     """
comments: "I have no experience with it, but you might try the unofficial open-source program (""Confluence Companion App for Linux"") that was posted in the comments of the above-referenced issue: https://github.com/<USER>companion-<USER>

summary: "Enable use of x-forwarded-for to log browser IP addresses"
description: """{panel:bgColor=#e7f4fa}    *NOTE:* This suggestion is for *Confluence Server*.    {panel}    h3. Problem    Confluence access logging is not able to log client IPs when a proxy is place. Load balancers and reverse proxy devices commonly use the """"x-forwarded-for"""" [HTTP header|https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers] to enable logging of the actual visitor IP address rather than the proxy IP address.    h3. Suggested Solution    Enable configuration of Confluence to log the IP address from the """"x-forwarded-for"""" header to the access logs.      h3. Workaround    Customers have reported that modifying the log4j configuration of Confluence is not a viable workaround. Fortunately, this can be achieved using the access logs that are enabled by default in the Tomcat container that ships with Confluence.    # Edit the file {{CONFLUENCE_INSTALL_DIR/conf/server.xml}}  # Insert this line before the line with """"AccessLogValve"""":   #* (!) Escape the """"."""" characters in PROXY_IP_ADDRESS with a backlash. Example: {{internalProxies=""""1\.1\.1\.8""""}}  # Configure AccessLogValve to accept the substituted IP from RemoteIpValve by adding the requestAttributesEnabled attribute to the AccessLogValve:     For full details see the Tomcat [RemoteIpValve documentation|https://tomcat.apache.org/tomcat-7.0-doc/api/org/apache/catalina/valves/RemoteIpValve.html]."""

summary: "User Management: Change Default Filter from 10 to 100 Users"
description: """Summary: Ability to change the default user filter from 10 users  h3. Problem    Under the _User Management_ > _Users_ option, if you change the filter from 10 to 100, navigate away and come back to this page, the user list defaults to 10 again.  h3. Suggested Solution    If we change the filter to display a number of users from 10 to any other number (20, 50, or 100), Confluence should be able to stick to those settings next time when the admin comes back to the _Users_ page.  h3. Why This Is Important    For a Confluence site with a large number of users, it can be ineffective for admins when Confluence always goes back to the default number 10, and they need to make changes to the list every time they visit the page.  h3. Workaround    N/A"""

summary: "As a Confluence Administrator I would like to import Spaces via REST API"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  As a Confluence administrator I am able to import a specific space through the admin UI as explained in [Restoring a Space|https://confluence.<USER>doc/restoring-a-space-152036.html].    Importing multiple spaces can take a long time thought the UI, since you need to perform the same task multiple times.  It would be interesting to have an API method to automate this task.    h3. Suggested Solution  Add a REST API method to import a Confluence Space.     h3. Workaround   Although the [SOAP-XML API|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/confluence-<USER>rpc-apis/] is deprecated, it is still available in Confluence while not all methods are ported to REST.    The Confluence administrator may use the [{{importSpace}} method|https://developer.<USER>server/confluence/remote-confluence-methods/#management] to import a Confluence Space.    Below is an example of such an operation using Python:  """

summary: "Allow Companion app to connect to Confluence without requiring infrastructure changes"
description: """Some of our customers are facing an issue where they cannot use the Companion App to edit files. They run Confluence in managed environments where proxies are employed and ports are often blocked. Their infrastructure teams may also have security protocols or policies they must abide by that forbid some of the access that Companion App requires to function.    This access is often something both the users and the infrastructure teams have no ability to change in their environment leading to an impaired experience and no ability to edit documents in this manner.    Since most browsers do not allow an insecure websocket connection (from the browser to Companion) when Confluence is hosted on a secure connection, in order to make a secure websocket connection between the browser and companion, we've created a DNS on the public internet for atlassian-domain-for-localhost-connections-only.com that maps to the IP address 127.0.0.1. It is imperative that this domain is excluded from the Browser's forward proxies, otherwise the Browser will never find its way from the forward proxy back to the Companion App on the local machine. If the connection is not allowed we do fall back to trying to connect to 127.0.0.1 directly if the domain connections failed, however depending on the cause of failure this could take additional time before a connection is made.     """
comments: "It would be helpful to have at least a fallback to 127.0.0.1 although I don't understand why this IP or ""localhost"" is not being used directly instead of the dummy hostname mapping to 127.0.0.1.    I wasn't able to see requests to 127.0.0.1 but requests to the dummy hostname only. After five requests resulting in 403 errors (that is after about 3 minutes) Confluence shows the installation dialogue for the Companion App although the app has already been installed. Even after that Confluence keeps creating requests to the dummy hostname. I was not able to observe any fallback to 127.0.0.1.    Kind Regards,    <USER>    " [SEP] "As Enterprise Company we will NOT add the DNS name atlassian-domain-for-localhost-connections-only.com to our company wide proxyconf Configuration. As users are not allowed to change their company wide proxy settings (that uses the company wide proxyconf) the companion app is not working for us, as the browser could not connect to the companion app.    Who in the hell at Atlassian h

summary: "Alloe admin to bulk update the page watcher"
description: """h3. Summary  Currently, Confluence does not have the feature to bulk adding user or replace using to watch pages in Confluence. Admin will need to open the page and add the user as a watcher of the page. It will be efficient if the admin is allowed to bulk adding user to watcher pages."""
comments: "To give some context to this.    Say a user ""employee.123"" watches 80 pages and spaces.    That employee changes role or leaves the company. A new user account ""employee.456"" is made for a new user.    As an admin, I wish to make ""employee.456"" a watcher of all the spaces and pages that ""employee.123"" watches. I wish to do this without having to select each page individually and add the new user as a watcher." [SEP] "Sounds like it could be solved with CONFSERVER-44953 which is about allowing groups to be defined as a watcher. Simply defining a user group for the role and then changing the member(s) of the group would solve this use case. But then, CONFSERVER-44953 is not (yet) implemented either... "

summary: "Possibility to change space icons' shape"
description: """h3. Problem Definition      * Confluence 6.5 showed the space icons, under the *My Spaces* section in the left sidebar, as circular shapes. In Confluence 6.15, these same icons are now showing up in square shapes. This change affected customer who had the icons designed to fit circular shapes.    h3. Suggested Solution    * Provide an option that allows users to select in which shape they would like to display the space icons.    h3. Why this is important       * In Confluence instances that have many spaces, changing their icons to match the now square shapes can be quite demanding since this isn't something possible to perform in bulk.    h3. Workaround     * N/A"""
comments: "Having the ability to switch back to the circular space icons of the Confluence 6.5 era would be great. We use Confluence spaces at our R&D company to capture the knowledge and files for each of our projects, one space per project, and to represent these projects across the company, particularly to senior management. We had designed some nice logos for each project, which fit into the circular layout. Unfortunately, the new square layout crops them slightly and doesn't really visually match what we had originally designed. Obviously, everything functionally still works great, but having a really nice presentation was great for us too. In addition, it also maintains the same visual look across our related Jira and Bitbucket project instances. It would be really nice if you could provide an option to return to the circular layout."

summary: "Allow the PDF Export Language Font to be set by each user's account"
description: """h3. Problem Definition      * Currently, it is only possible to change the PDF Export language font to the whole Confluence instance, but it might be useful for some use cases to have the PDF Export language font more user-centered, allowing each user to have one specific to their use.    h3. Suggested Solution     * Have an option to change the PDF Export language font for one user only, not to the whole instance    h3. Why this is important       * In teams that work with multiple languages, it might be helpful to have this feature set individually, allowing each person to have its own set of language and font, depending on their needs.    h3. Workaround   N/A"""

summary: "Increase managed Synchrony JVM maximum memory to at least 2GB"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  By default, in the managed Synchrony deployment (both for Server and Data Center), the default Synchrony heap is 1GB.  This is not enough for most of the deployments and it may often cause OOM problems on the Synchrony JVM.    On Confluence Data Center prior to the managed Synchrony, the startup script had the heap configured to 2GB by default.  Migrating to managed from the standalone cluster may create a problem if the default configuration is used.    h3. Suggested Solution  Ship Confluence (Server and Data Center) with a default Synchrony JVM heap size to at least 2GB by using the following JVM attribute:      Also, please consider if a higher value (such as 4GB) should be used as the default heap size on Data Center.    h3. Workaround   Manually configure the {{-Dsynchrony.memory.max=2g}} in each Confluence node.  See [How to Increase Synchrony Heap Allocation|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-increase-synchrony-heap-allocation-936516650.html] and [Setting properties and options on startup|https://confluence.<USER>adminjiraserver/setting-properties-and-options-on-startup-938847831.html] for further information."""

summary: "Ability to track a space or page email notification and whether it was triggered by auto-watch or manual watch"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Confluence currently provides the ability to watch a specific page or an entire Space. You can do that either via auto-watch or a manual watch.  At the moment, it is not possible to track whether an email notification was generated through Space or Page watch and whether this was triggered through auto-watch or a manual watch      h3. Suggested Solution  It would be good if we are able to distinguish those scenarios which will allow the Confluence admin to determine where the bulk of the email notifications is coming from."""

summary: "Would like to limit attachment size in Confluence per file type"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    We have in the past seen issues with the Content Indexing processing and application degradation due to large mp4 video attachments being viewed or downloaded. We attempted to alleviate this by decreasing the attachment size from default to 526.84 MB. This seemed to work for some time until recently where we have had two occurrences of a user viewing an mp4 attachment and it subsequently causing a block on our content indexing queue.   For each occurrence once the video was closed out the Content Indexing started processing again.  h3. Suggested Solution    We actually have 2 suggestions to improve the way we work with attachments that would help in this case:   # Would like to limit the attachments size per file type, so we can limit the size of videos being attached.   # Would like to be able to choose which file types can be uploaded or which should be restricted without using a third-party plugin for that.    h3. Why this is important    To avoid issues with the Content Indexing processing and application degradation due to large mp4 video attachments being viewed or downloaded.  h3. Workaround   * Use third-party plugins to restrict file types from being attached."""

summary: "Managing Confluence for Enterprise Support"
description: """Hello Team,    Background of the Confluence Setup -    Currently we have Confluence Setup on-premises with the data center licence running based on the Docker + Kubernetes on AWS Instances.    Suggestion Needed on the below questions   * We want to configure Confluence to handle more 50K user at at time, please suggest what could be best approach?   * We want to perform the load testing before releasing to production, do you suggest some options to handle it?   * What could be the best configuration for each Docker POD running on the AWS instance type m5.2x large?     """

summary: "Improve installation process by preventing any other request to brake installation."
description: """If we start installation process and after entering the license information and during the DB setup, we try to open Confluence instance, we will get an error 500 messages and DB will get corrupted.    The only solution, in that case, would be to destroy the data in db, delete confluence.cfg.xml and restart the instance.    This can happen due to multiple reasons, like monitoring tool, or load balancer checks.    Therefore it would be good to have a safety mechanism, that would redirect the Confluence request, for example, based on session, to a static page until installation is finished."""

summary: "Related Labels: Exclude Label(s)"
description: """Hello, I would like an """"exclude label(s)"""" property be added to related labels.    Here's why:    First, I have a situation where templates are marked with a label themselves. This means that all pages instantiated from the template share that label. We'll call them """"Cars"""" and """"Planes"""".    I need to do this, because I have pages that run page properties reports on each type, respectively. So far, so good.    However, the template itself has a related-labels macro in it, so that readers can see which cars and planes are associated with each other. This doesn't work so well, because, since all Cars share a Cars label, they are all implicitly related. That's not so good.    I might also have been able to make the current system work if Related Labels had a """"count"""" option. For example, if I could have said """"2,"""" then the template property would have been removed implicitly.               """
comments: "Update for clarity. I am using related labels with no labels, meaning all labels related to those on the page. The difficulty occurs when the template stamps in a common label, which does not mean that the page is really related to other pages that have that label (or, I don't want it to be, anyway)"

summary: "Lots of SQL queries issued for REST GET Content "
description: """h3. Problem Definition    Lot of Content search queries as below are issued when accessed via REST content api.  {code:sql}  select abstractpa0_.CONTENTID as CONTENTI1_12_, .... from CONTENT abstractpa0_   ... limit 500, 501;  select abstractpa0_.CONTENTID as CONTENTI1_12_, .... from CONTENT abstractpa0_   ... limit 1000, 501;  select abstractpa0_.CONTENTID as CONTENTI1_12_, .... from CONTENT abstractpa0_   ... limit 1500, 501;  .....  select abstractpa0_.CONTENTID as CONTENTI1_12_, .... from CONTENT abstractpa0_   ... limit 10000, 501;  {code}  h3. How to replicate this problem    There are at least two types of scenario to cause this problem –   *Scenario 1*   # Create a Confluence Instance with many content. e.g. 20,000 content.   # Create a user who has access permission to the contents created in step 1. e.g. testuser   # Clear cache   # Send a GET request with {{rest/api/content?start=<any number over 500>}} to the instance. e.g. start from 10,000  {code:java}  curl -u testuser:testuser http://<confluence-base-url>/rest/api/content?start=10000  {code}    i.e. Confluence tries to fetch all 10,500 contents to obtain just 500 contents from 10,001st to 10,500th items.    *Scenario 2*   # Create a Confluence Instance with many content. e.g. 20,000 content.   # Create a user who *doesn't* have access permission to the contents created in step 1. e.g. testuser   # Clear cache   # Send a GET request with {{rest/api/content}} to the instance.  {code:java}  curl -u testuser:testuser http://<confluence-base-url>/rest/api/content  {code}    i.e. Confluence tries to fetch all 20,000 contents to filter the contents on which the testuser has permission.  h3. Suggested Solution    For scenario 1, to obtain data for {{rest/api/content?start=10000}} , *only* the query  {code:java}  select abstractpa0_.CONTENTID as CONTENTI1_12_, .... from CONTENT abstractpa0_   ... limit 10000, 501;  {code}  should be issued.    For scenario 2, filter the CONTENT first with permission and fetch the items later.  h3. Why this is important    Because this can cause a performance problem on database server processing a lot of queries at the same time."""

summary: "No notification regarding Application Link With Same Consumer Keys"
description: """h3. Problem    There is no notification that using the same Consumer Key with multiple application links won't work.    h3. Suggested Solution    _Add a notification or block users from using a Consume key that is already in use with another application link._   h3. Why This Is Important    _When users aren't aware that this is the case, they might create multiple application links for different purposes, using the same consumer key. This results in user frustration and confusion as to why it's not working as expected._   h3. Workaround  {quote}_""""....It is expected that """"Consumer Key""""s will be unique across Application Links. This is because a """"Consumer Key"""" uniquely identifies each """"client"""" to Jira in much the same way that a """"username"""" might be uniquely associated with a user. If they are duplicated/reused then multiple clients are saying that they are the same client and that is what leads to the behaviour observed when updating the Application Links.""""_    _-_Matthew ????? Knox (https://jira.<USER>browse/JRACLOUD-74475)  {quote}"""
comments: "You do get an error if you try to create two application links with the same link URL. A similar error for duplicate Consumer Keys would be great." [SEP] "This would add a ton of value. (y)"

summary: "External collaborators role"
description: """Add a new user role called ‘External Collaborators’, where admins can add external users to specific spaces and have confidence that they won't be able to see anything else on the site, or things like the people directory, settings, or using mentions. The ‘External Collaborator’ role would be required to log into the Confluence Server/Data Center instance but would not count against the licensing limit."""

summary: "Implement ""/<ID>/descendant/page"" Rest API endpoint on Confluence server"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    At the moment, there is no method available to get all children pages from a parent page, including all levels of the tree. Only direct children are returned from the existing methods.    h3. Suggested Solution    Implement the same endpoints that are available on Confluence Cloud:    * [Get content descendants|https://developer.<USER>cloud/confluence/rest/#api-content-id-descendant-get]  * [Get content descendants by type|https://developer.<USER>cloud/confluence/rest/#api-content-id-descendant-type-get]    Both requests are available on server instances already. However, the first one only returns attachments and comments. The second one returns the following error if accessed:  * /rest/api/content/<ID>/descendant/page      h3. Why this is important    Making those methods available would greatly reduce the effort to build the full page tree using Rest API.     h3. Workaround     At the moment, the only option is to recursively navigate through the tree, one level at a time.  """

summary: "Creating a Jira Issue from Confluence may be slow depending on the number of existing projects in the Jira instance"
description: """h3. Issue Summary  When Confluence is integrated with Jira, the Confluence user can create Jira issues directly from Confluence as explained in the [product documentation|https://confluence.<USER>doc/use-jira-applications-and-confluence-together-427623543.html#UseJiraapplicationsandConfluencetogether-CreateissuesfrominsideConfluence].    Loading the create issue form, either on viewing or editing a page, may be slow while Confluence tries to get the the avatar for all projects in the target Jira instance, sending a request for each project from the user's browser to Confluence and then to Jira.    If the Jira instance has just a few projects, then it completes quickly, without the user noticing any delay.    If Confluence is connected to a large Jira instance, with thousands of projects, then it may take some seconds (or even minutes) to release the UI so the user can continue the process to create the Jira Issue.  If the Jira instance has 3,000 projects, then 3,000 requests will be created from the browser to Confluence, no matter if the project is not appearing in the screen.    A better design approach would be one of the two options:  # Use paginated requests from the _select_ box.   #* This would decrease the number of requests to Jira to only those projects the user is viewing in the UI.   #* For example, [select2|https://select2.org/data-sources/ajax] has this feature.  # Don't load the project avatar on the form.      Since Confluence would be processing thousands of requests from an user (or multiple users, leaning on how much this functionality is used), depending how fast it can process or drop these requests, there will be a lot of requests queued on Tomcat or on the HTTP server (if using a reverse proxy).  This can give other users the feeling that Confluence is not working or is slow, since new requests will also be queued.    h3. Steps to Reproduce   # Install a vanilla instance of Jira.    #* Jira *8.5.3* was used to validate the issue.   # Create 2,000 sample projects.    #* You may use the following shell script to create these projects.    # Install a vanilla instance of Confluence.   #* The issue was validated on Confluence versions *6.13.10*, *7.2.1* and *7.4.0*.  # Create an application link between Confluence and Jira.   #* Although it doesn't change anything on the bug behavior, create the link using _impersonation_ so you don't need to allow authentication between applications.  # Create a sample Space in Confluence.  # Access the home page of the sample Space.  # Highlight a text on the page and click on the _Create Jira Issue_ icon to open the form.  # On the _Create Task_ form click on the _Select project_ box.    h3. Expected Results  Confluence quickly loads the options in the select box.    h3. Actual Results  There will be a delay to have the select box opened and the browser may freeze for some seconds while thousands of requests are created the sent to Confluence.    If browser's developer tools is opened during the process, monitoring the network requests will shows 2,000 (or more depending on the number of projects) requests.   !screenshot-001.png|thumbnail!        The requests are similar to      If there's an access log enabled on front of Jira (either on a reverse proxy or on Tomcat), there will be lots of requests similar to the below:        Some of these requests may fail depending on the load on Confluence or on Jira -- if Confluence and Jira are capable of dealing with all the requests, then these errors won't occur.  The request may fail with an HTTP 500 status and with a {{JiraCommunicationException}} error.  Looking at the Confluence application logs we may find entries similar to the below:      If another user tries to display a page from Confluence while it is still processing the requests for the projects' avatars, then this new request may take a long time to be processed, since it will be queued on the Tomcat or Reverse Proxy side.  Depending on the timeout configuration of the Reverse Proxy, this new request may not even reach the Confluence server.    The same occurs if the user tries to create the Jira issue using the Jira Issues Macro when editing a page.   !screenshot-002.png|thumbnail!              h3. Workaround  _Currently there is no known workaround for this behavior. A workaround will be added here when available._"""

summary: "Cache the information of the Confluence Usage Stats"
description: """h3. Problem Definition     * The *Confluence Usage Stats* plugin is known to cause performance problems in Confluence, especially in large instances. Some users need to use their information, though, and the information it retrieves is loaded dynamically when accessing a page where the plugin macros are at.    h3. Suggested Solution      * Provide a feature that can retrieve the information from the *Confluence Usage Stats* plugin and cache it, instead of dynamically load it. The time frame of the stored content can be defined by the user.    h3. Why this is important       * This prevents the performance problem caused by this plugin and allows the users to use it as well.    h3. Workaround   N/A"""

summary: "Provide the ability to use macros within Confluence Questions"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Currently, it is only possible to use Confluence macros within Confluence itself, but not within Confluence Questions    h3. Suggested Solution  Provide the ability to embed macros into questions and answers in Confluence Questions."""

summary: "Create an XML file cleaner"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    * There are currently some known problems with an XML file from Confluence as the one described in our ['Incorrect string value' error thrown when restoring XML backup in Confluence|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/incorrect-string-value-error-thrown-when-restoring-xml-backup-in-confluence-777009599.html] document, for example. Those types of problems are very time-consuming for our customers.    h3. Suggested Solution    * Create an alternative to clean the XML files of Confluence from known problems with them before attempting to import them in another Confluence instance.    h3. Why this is important     * This can avoid quite a few cases where there is an incorrect data in the XML file itself.    h3. Workaround   * Follow the workarounds of each document available.    h3. Notes  * Other examples of documents in which a script can fix the error identified in the XML file:  ** [Confluence site XML import fails with """"duplicate key"""" error|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/confluence-site-xml-import-fails-with-duplicate-key-error-438993638.html]  ** [Confluence Space import failed with 'Unable to complete import: Error while importing backup: For input string: """"""""'|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/confluence-space-import-failed-with-unable-to-complete-import-error-while-importing-backup-for-input-string-998893140.html]"""
comments: "yeah, that will be intersting" [SEP] "This is a very good idea. While we understand that Atlassian recommends to rely on native DB backups for large instances, it would be great if there is a cleaner file that can fix the issues with the large backup xml file. Atlassian applications are being widely used for several years now. They should consider that the usage/size of the backup files would  have increased (in several 10s of GBs) and inbuilt back up strategy like xml should be more dynamic, intact and the import should be less time consuming. " [SEP] "+1    Last week I did a  Confluence backup xml that ran for 48 hours in a medium size customer, it failed to import due ""An invalid XML character"", only after using the atlassian-xml-cleaner-0.1.jar it worked;"

summary: "Bulk instead of individual e-mails from Confluence"
description: """+As a+ system admin  +I want+ Confluence to send notifications in bulk  +So that+ E-Mail servers are not overloaded with lots of individual e-mails, one for each update for each recipient, whenever a page gets updated.    h3. Problem Definition  Customers may face e-mail storms from Confluence due to notifications being sent for pages and blogs watched.
