As Confluence generates one individual e-mail for each user, this can escalate to several users being notified for several pages, causing a heavy load on e-mail servers.    h3. Suggested Solution  Bulk e-mail notifications so that instead of one message per user, it could send one message for the total watchers or grouping them for configurable number if the watcher list is large, for instance, one message for each 30 watchers. (BCC them?)    h3. Workaround  None at the moment, but you can consider:  * Reducing the events notified:  [How to globally disable e-mail notifications with page content|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-globally-disable-e-mail-notifications-with-page-content-718670714.html]  * Increasing the time for batching e-mails:  [Email Notifications|https://confluence.<USER>doc/email-notifications-145162.html?_ga=2.217851798.1001520385.1583151685-296208751.1582325698]"""

summary: "Add option to strip images from email notifications "
description: """Currently, email notifications do not include any images or other attachments. Instead, an empty box is shown with a question <USER> This is confusing and visually unattractive. Besides, it gives end users the impression that Confluence is not working properly.    People have been complaining about this for years, both in the Confluence and JIRA forums. See e.g. [this Confluence thread |https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-9443?focusedCommentId=426627&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels%3Acomment-tabpanel#comment-426627] and [this JIRA thread|https://jira.<USER>browse/JRASERVER-41675]    So why not add an option to the mail server configuration settings under Administration > General Configuration > Mail Servers, to strip all images / attachments from email notifications? This would be an elegant solution for end users (who would rather see no images at all than broken images) and also means no complex solution has to be built for handling permissions to download / view images from restricted Confluence posts or pages.  """

summary: "REST API needs ability to remove specific versions of a page"
description: """h3. Problem statement  The REST API methods in our [documentation|https://docs.<USER>ConfluenceServer/rest/6.11.1/] doesn't have a method for deleting a specific version of a piece of content.    If we run this command to remove an older version of a page, it returns 404 although the page exists and I have permission to delete it.      If we change the ID to the latest version of the page, then the content is removed.    h3. Suggestion  Provide a new method for removing specific versions of a content or modify the existing one so that it doesn't return a 404 for a valid content ID."""
comments: "This also affects our Confluence instance with more than 90.000 users.    We could not agree more with this request.    Why does the method return 404 for valid content ids?    It is such a big housekeeping use-case to delete old versions (and not the newest) of content.(e.g. older than x years getting easily from the database)    Would be really great if this does not return 404 for valid content id's:  [https://docs.<USER>ConfluenceServer/rest/6.11.1/#content-delete]     "

summary: "Add documentation for sending logs to an Elastic server"
description: """h3. Problem    It would be nice if we had documentation for sending logs to an Elastic Server.  h3. Suggested Solution    **Add documentation for sending logs to an Elastic server.       h3. Why This Is Important    It would increase the user's ability to manage how their logs are stored, empowering our users to better document their logs for future auditing.   h3. Workaround    _None as of this writing._ """
comments: "Current implementation has multiple datacenter nodes and multiple logs.   Would prefer to decentralized log this information to a single ELK stack somewhere, so a documented log4j entry would be quite helpful here."

summary: "Changes for Cache Performance Tuning page"
description: """h2. Issue Summary    Our [Cache Performance Tuning|https://confluence.<USER>doc/cache-performance-tuning-169119133.html] page, currently states the following:  {quote}It is essential for the administrator of a large production installation of Confluence to size the caches to suit its environment.  {quote}  This can lead Administrators into tuning cache performance for cache performance's sake, without measuring what effect this has on customers/page loads. Thus we have no idea how effective the changes are, and much more importantly, if they are needed.  h2. Request    We've made the following addition to the page, just so we can better inform our customers.  {quote}(i) While this is an important task, having a comparison point is essential to help identify how effective the changes are, and much more importantly, if they are needed. (e.g. Comparing page load time, login time, <USER>).  {quote}  We'd like to have the changes made for all current and future versions of the page."""

summary: "New Macro Button to Add / Remove Labels to a Page (Applications within Confluence)"
description: """I have a comment and some votes on a page about the Create from Template button and labels. But what I am proposing is so easy for you to do but also so powerful that I decided to create a ticket for it to pitch the idea to you.     I'd like to see the ability to have a configurable button on a Confluence page that you can have the option to only add a label to the page, only remove a label to a page or choose that it toggles the said label on/off of the page.          This functionality would allow for Application like functionality within Confluence. Here is a few examples below.          Case Scenario 1:  I have a page that I use the create from template button to make pages that are storyboards for eLearning training development lessons. The new storyboard page inherits the labels from my template page. I have a work flow main page based on labels and what labels are on the storyboard pages. I am using the macro Content by label to show the storyboards in each state. To advance a storyboard from planned, to in production, to editing, <USER> I simply remove a label to move it from one step in the work flow to the next. With buttons that can add or remove labels then I can have a toolbar in my page template to move a page through the workflow. This would be powerful for the end user.          Case Scenario 2:  I have a knowledge base page with a create from template button to create a new how-to article. The new pages inherit the label """"draft"""" from my template page. I now have draft how-to articles that myself and my tech writer can vet out and polish up and then remove the draft label and consider the page as production.          Theoretical Case Scenarios: Using the concept I am employing above you can have a page for """"ToDo"""". You can create your tasks for yourself and merely press a button on that tasks page as """"Done"""" and you're done. That main page could easily be copied and setup for other people for their own task list without much explanation of what labels do what for the status of your task list. Also it would be customization for power users obviously.     Also, you could have a team of people creating new projects and then have them go through a work flow to completion. End users would have to just click a button on a page that is in plain English. This would be such an amazing and powerful feature for your company to have. Companies don't want to have to purchase very expensive line of business applications for the various project management or process management that they do. For most uses this would serve the purpose needed.          A complementary macro to the button or perhaps the macro could have an alternate option of a drop down that could be configurable so you can present 3 labels (statuses) for your particular workflow to your end users.         You guys have nothing to lose and everything to gain from this. Can I get it next week? : )"""
comments: "Some people were asking for this feature along with me on these other tickets but these other tickets are really for Create from Template feature to have the option to add a different label than what is being inherited from the template. (Which needs to be done too FYI because we that will make our templates for multiple uses).  https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-30716  https://jira.<USER>browse/CONFSERVER-29581"

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I'd like to have an option to see the status of index replication across Confluence nodes"
description: """h3. Problem Definition    As a Confluence administrator, I'd like to have an option to see the status of index replication between Confluence nodes.    h3. Suggested Solution    In Jira, there is the option to [check the status of the index|https://docs.<USER>software/jira/docs/api/REST/8.4.2/#api/2/index/summary-getIndexSummary] by using the {{/rest/api/2/index/summary/}} API call for each node and then compare the results.    It would be helpful to have a similar option in Confluence.    h3. Why this is important    Currently, there is no way for administrators to determine that the index across the nodes is staying in sync.    h3. Workaround  Confluence administrator may rely on a manual validation as described in [this KB|https://confluence.<USER>confkb/how-to-manually-rebuild-content-index-from-scratch-on-confluence-data-center-with-downtime-1019380452.html#HowtomanuallyrebuildcontentindexfromscratchonConfluenceDataCenterwithdowntime-Confirmingcontentindexisworkingonallnodes]."""

summary: "As a Confluence administrator I would like to have an admin page to view global user sessions"
description: """h3. Problem Definition  Jira has an admin page to view global user sessions as detailed in [Monitor a user's activity|https://confluence.<USER>adminjiraserver072/monitor-a-user-s-activity-828787672.html].  It would be interesting to have something similar on Confluence too.  As listed in [this KB|https://confluence.<USER>adminjiraserver072/monitor-a-user-s-activity-828787672.html], benefits are:  {quote}   * Know who to contact before planned downtime.   * Regularly monitor the average number of active users to evaluating your licensing quota.   * View user count if the system is under duress.   * And more!  {quote}    h3. Suggested Solution  Implement on Confluence an admin page similar to the _Current User Sessions in JIRA_ ({{/secure/admin/CurrentUsersList.jspa}}).     h3. Workaround   _There's no workaround so far_"""
